## Introduction

#### What is this game?

Digimon Story: Time Stranger is the latest entry in the digimon Story series. A series of digimon games which focus primarily on story between humans and digimon.

It is not a sequal of Cyber sleuth or any previous games. It is also the first large scale digimon game to have an english dub. With over 450 digimon that can join you on the adventure Time stranger boasts the largest arsenal of mons available to players.

The gameplay and combat is standard turn based RPG fair, if you are familiar with SMT, Persona or any turn based game you'll be right at home for this one.

The main plot of Time stranger revolves around an agent from organization Adamas searching for anomalies related to digimon and ends up sent to the past before the events of a great catastrophe, there he encounter Inori Misono and Aegiomon, a human and her partner digimon who may alter the course of history itself.

#### What this guide will cover?

This guide aims to be a full guide including information and data on all digimon in the game, a walkthrough of the main campaign and side quests. It will cover basically anything a player needs in terms of resources for when playing the game.

#### For folks new to Digimon

For those of you new to digimon, with an interest in the game, worrying about whether you need to be digimon savvy to enjoy it. Have no fear most if not all digimon entries in games and anime are stand alone and hence may have different rules and settings (though there are some constants such as the digital world and various digimon), so even if you are new this game is a perfect entry point.

![](/ffaq/40/1/)

## Personality Guide

#### Video Guide

Below is a Video Guide version of this page. 

#### Introduction

Much like Cyber sleuth before it Time Stranger features a personality system, which is also somewhat tied to the DigiFarm.

Unlike Cyber sleuth which featured only a small number of personalities which only somewhat affected stats in small if not insignificant way, Time Stranger takes the system a step further.

The personalities in Time Strangerr are closely tied to how a digimon gains stats upon leveling up.

In Cyber sleuth a digimon's growth was more or less fixed with personality only letting you add a bit extra on top, and there were other limitations to that.

Time stranger gives the player a lot more freedom. When you convert a new digimon it will randomly have 1 of 16 possible personalities.  
These personalities define which stats are given priority when leveling up.

The game even shows you this in the personality menu for each individual digimon:

![](/ffaq/40/301/)

#### Core and True Personality Types

In Time Stranger there are 4 core personality types, Valor, Amicability, Philanthropy and Wisdom.

Each corresponds to a different stat which will be grown when leveling up, these are the main stats focused on by the personality.

![](/ffaq/40/304/)![](/ffaq/40/303/)

  * Phlanthropy focus on Spirit (Magic Defense)
  * Wisdom on Intelligence (Magic Attack)
  * Valor on Strength/Attack
  * and Amicability on Defense



As I mentioned before there are 16 personalities in this game not 4, within each of these 4 Core Personalites are 4 true personalites each:

![](/ffaq/40/305/)

Each true personality will grow an additional stat to a lesser degree alongside the main core stat.

For example a Daring Digimon will gain more ATK and DEF stats as they level up (with ATK growing more than DEF).   
Note: These statt gains will appear tiny early on but will balloon as you unlock Agent Skills of the related tree.

#### Changing Personality and DigiFarm

You can freely change the personality of your digimon throughout the game with no limitations, there are 2 ways of doing so admittedly you will not be able to use them effectively until mid to late game.

The first way to change your digimon's personality is by speaking to them while walking around with them in the digital world or dungeons.

However, your answers to their questions and decisions will alter their personality slightly. Doing this you can guide them towards a given personality slowly, the game will also show you what affect your answer will have (in terms of direction).

That said this method is slow and is only good if you're taking the slow and steady path.

The 2nd method is much better and relatively faster depending on where you are in the game.

By training digimon in the digifarm, your choice of training and training item used will change their personality.

![](/ffaq/40/306/)

There are 3 types of Training Items, A, B and C. A types change personality slightly depending on the stat, B types change them greatly  
lastly C types do not change personality at all (only for stats).

Generally training takes 15 minutes to 90 minutes to complete depending on type and agent skills you have unlocked.  
However, any training can be instantly completed by paying 10000 yen, which is a lot early on, however by Mid to Endgame becomes easily available and thus personality changes can be made instantly. Even with the time issue it is still the relative faster method.

New training items can be crafted at Zudomon's blacksmith shop in Central Town or Rebellion Village, depending on where you are in the game.

Training different stats moves the personality in a different direction based on the grid shown above.

Generally the chart reads: Affection as Up, Understanding as Left, Heart as Right and Companionship as Down.

The below table breaks down how each training type affects personality:

Training Method| Personality Change| Training Item  
---|---|---  
Stats| Training Set| Personality Shift| Real Direction| Type A| Type B| Type C  
HP| Sauna| Heart| Right| Slight Change| Great Change| No Change  
SP| Boombox| Understanding| Left  
ATK| Punching Bag| Heart and Affection| Right and Up  
DEF| Club| Heart and Companionship (a little)| Right and Down (a little)  
INT| Classroom| Understanding and Companionship| Left and Down  
SPI| Tearoom| Understanding and Affection| Left and Up  
SPD| Treadmill| Heart (a little) and Companionship| Right (a little) and Down  
  
#### Personality Skills

Each digimon when converted has a Personality Skill, based on the Personality it was born with.

A Personality skill is a passive ability which can aid the digimon in battle in small and big ways depending on the skills. Not all these skills are equal some are very good and others are pretty useless. Digimon can only learn 1 skill at a time.

There are a total of 37 different Personality Skills in the game. When a digimon changes their personality they can learn a new personality type based on the new personality.

Each of the core personality types has 2 default personality skills the digimon can learn these are usually minor skills which occur with common frequency when chaging personalities.

On the other hand, each of the 16 true personalities allow the digimon to learn 2 unique skills of varying rarity.

Thus when a digimon changes personality there are 4 possible skills they can learn, of which one will be displayed and the player can choose whether to swap their current personality skill for it or not. (Changing Personality skill is optional and not required when changing personalities).

For example a digimon changing personality from Devoted to Zealous, can learn: Warrior Wisdom, Song of Triumph, First in Line or Fast Break.

Which skill it can learn however is completely random, to make matters worse some skills are rarer and harder to get than others.  
So you may find yourself changing personalities a lot to get certain personality skills (which can be expensive).

The below graphic shows the personality skills learnable by each personality.

![](/ffaq/40/321/)

#### Personality Skill List

![](/ffaq/40/322/)![](/ffaq/40/326/)![](/ffaq/40/325/)![](/ffaq/40/324/)![](/ffaq/40/323/)

#### Personality Skill Recommendations

This section is a recommendation of Personality Skills based on space244s and my own experiences:

  1. Combo Magic(Always triggers at over 100% HP), if combined with Great Embrace on a different digimon, it essentially  
allows the digimon to spam magic attacks twice in a row. **Be warned that this is a bug and may be patched!**
  2. Planning Ahead(20% debuff to Def and Spi at 100 Bond)  
or   
Cheer (20% buff to Atk and Int at 100 Bond)  
When having 3 on each digimon with the skill the effect will stack for a maximum of 60%. This is a great way to buff your party or debuff the enemy.
  3. Booing, extends the use of debuff skill, this is very handy against bosses who get a lot of turns so they don't lose their debuff by your next turn. Does not work on skills which cause Debuffs as an extra effect (such as attack special skills) it can only be used with stat down skills.
  4. Haymaker  
If you have a mon with a high crit rate, be it from skills, equipment, and/or High Bond you can equip this to increase the crit damage by 20%.
  5. Great Embrace  
A great skill to combine with Personality Skills which improve stats at high HP (or with Combo Magic while it's still bugged).  
It doubles the MAX HP of those healed by the user, this can allow the entire party to have 19998 HP max, a great end game or Mega+ set up.
  6. SP Supplement: This skill isn't useful in most circumstance. On Mega+ you cannot use items to recover SP, so it may be useful with digimon who use skills which consume all their SP.
  7. Stout Strength, Defense Spirit.  
When combined with Great Embrace, this skill can greatly increase that stat of the digimon.  
The effect will cause an overflow even if the mon's stat is 9999.  
  
For Physical Attackers Stout Strength will allow them to deal more damage.  
  
For Magic/Physical Tanks Stout Spirit and Defense will increase their defenses so they'll take very little damage. Possibly even 0.



Other skills have some utility here and there but the above are my personal recommendations.

(Once again thank you to space244 for doing a lot of the testing).

#### Agent Skills

As you clear main quests and side quests you gain anomaly points to spend on Agent skills which can grant passive stat boosts and other effects to your digimon.

This is covered more in depth in the [Agent Skills](agent-skills) section of this guide.

Among the Agent skills are 4 tress which affect digimon of the 4 core personality types as shown below:

![](/ffaq/40/307/)

Each tree has the same 46 nodes. 21 of these nodes grant a buff to a given stat for digimon of that Core personality.

![](/ffaq/40/320/)![](/ffaq/40/319/)![](/ffaq/40/318/)![](/ffaq/40/317/)![](/ffaq/40/316/)![](/ffaq/40/315/)![](/ffaq/40/314/)

Each Body Boost Node has 3 nodes which grant 20%,25% and 30% for a total of 75% when all of them are unlocked.

For example: If in the above image I unlocked a Body Boost Lvl 1: ATK node (Sword), and had a digimon from any Amicability Personality, that digimon's base ATK stat would be increased by 20% permanently. Any gains from Training will also be increased by 20% as will any growths from leveling.

In this way the player can increase the stats of all digimon of a specific core personality by 75% once all these Body Boost is unlocked.

There are also 4 Perfection nodes on each Tree which cost 100 Anomaly points which grant an addition 25% to 2 specific stats for a SPECIFIC personality (not core personality), Sociable Perfection will for example give a 25% buff to ATK and SPI of Sociable digimon.

![](/ffaq/40/313/)

Other nodes, will lower the stats needed by digivolution by 60% (all nodes), increase experience gained based on Agent Rank, affect Training Time/Personality shift degree during training or grant other buffs to make raising those digimon easier.

![](/ffaq/40/312/)![](/ffaq/40/311/)![](/ffaq/40/310/)![](/ffaq/40/308/)

Each of the 16 personalities has a unique ability which can be unlocked in their respective trees.

For example: Unlocking Brave Blow in the Valor tree, will allow the digimon with the Brave Personality to ignore 3% of the target's DEF for each Agent rank (for a maximum of 30%).

![](/ffaq/40/309/)

I won't cover all those here as you have easy access to them in game, and in the Agent Section of the guide.

This section is more a reminder to be mindful how you spend your Anomaly points when raising digimon or aiming for specific personalities.

That's everything you need to know about personalities, to make raising digimon easier

## Digimon Grinding Guides

#### Grinding in Time Stranger

Digimon Time stranger takes an interesting approach to it's mechanics and stats, and as a result the exact methods for grinding are not as simple as beating enemies quickly or using EXP boosting items for fast gains.

Depending on the goal you are striving for you will find yourself doing different things here and there and in a lot of cases different methods work for different people.

This section will cover ALL of the best ways to grind in Time Stranger.

I will not be including AFK methods, because those while usable are rather slow. I should also mention it is possible some of these methods MAY become obsolete in future patches.

## Digimon Grinding Guides

### Digimon Leveling Guide

#### Leveling Up any digimon in 90 seconds to 2 minutes

 **Note: Level plays a role in damage dealt by digimon, higher level always = more damage!**

 **Thank you space244 for helping me come up with this method.**  
  
---  
  
Above is a video guide if you need video guidance.

This is THE fastest way to level up your digimon, however it requires some preparation before you can start doing it.

 **You need Agent Rank 7 to even begin this method, as you need access to Mega Levels.  
** **It is also advised you have the following nodes unlocked on the Loyalty and Valor Agent Skill Trees:**

  *  **Body Boost: Champion**
  *  **Body Boost: Ultimate**
  *  **Body Boost: ATK (Level 1,2 and 3)**
  *  **Body Boost: SPD (Level 1,2 and 3)**
  *  **Body Boost: HP (as many as you can)**
  *  **Brave Perfection**



##### Step 1: Infinite ExVeemon Exploit, Creating Stingmon

A well known exploit in the game, is that when you reach Mega level and devolve into a digimon that is DNA evolution for example Paildramon or Dinobeemon etc, when you return to the DNA evolution you can split them into 2 mons.

In this way the player can create duplicate digimon endlessly under the right conditions.

If you have met the above Agent Skill and Rank requirements then we can begin.

First we need to create a Stingmon with a Brave Personality and 100 Talent.

How you obtain the Stingmon is up to you. You can create one by converting a FunBeemon (at 200% for more Talent) and then evolving it to Stingmon. Wormmon is the other option. Regardless just get a Stingmon.

Use the DigiFarm to change it's personality to Brave (if it isn't already) and then increase it's talent.

If you have Talent chips go ahead and feed it to him, otherwise the fastest method is through Loading.

By Loading other digimon into Stingmon you can permanently increase his Talent, depending on the total talent of the loaded digimon.

 **Calculation for Talent:**  
 **When loading, a digimon receives 1/20th of the talent of the loaded digimon.**  
 **Loading a digimon with 100 talent gives 5 Talent, for example.**  
---  
  
 **My Method for reaching 100 Talent**

This is what I do because I find this generally faster. And this will serve as a tutorial of the evolution loop.

Because of the Agent skills we have Stingmon can instantly evolve. Follow the below loop:

 **Stingmon - > JewelBeemon -> TigerVespamon -> Dinobeemon -> Stingmon + ExVeemon  
**

The new Stingmon then becomes JewelBeemon and repeats the loop, this way we create several ExVeemon.

Feed Stingmon the ExVeemon you created and this will increase the Talent, Stingmon will also gain 1 Talent per loop.

Total Time needed for Creating new Stingmon and reaching Talent 100 = 8 to 10 minutes  
(Loading 5 ExVeemon at a time, took about 3 to 4 sets total)

That's the end of Step 1.

##### Step 2: Increasing Load Experience

Now this is what makes this method so fast, many people don't know how the Experience when Loading is calculated.

 **Load Experience = Generation + 10% of All Experience Digimon Earned Ever**  
---  
  
Generation refers to the digimon's evolution stage and acts as the base experience when loading a freshly converted mon:

Generation| Load Exp  
---|---  
In-Training 1| 10  
  
In-Training 2

| 100  
Rookie| 1000  
Champion| 7500  
Ultimate| 20000  
Mega| 50000  
Mega+| 100000  
  
The above numbers don't actually matter for what we are going to do but this is just to help you understand.

The important part is the **10% of All Experience Digimon Earned Ever,** this means a digimon can give an immense amount of experience if it has EARNED an immense amount of experience.

Furthermore when defusing a DNA Digimon like Dinobeemon, both created digimon have the same talent, bond AND LOAD EXPERIENCE.

Thus if we were to have Stingmon earn a very large amount of experience, then it would be possible to use the duplicated ExVeemon to near instantly level up a digimon to 99.

There is however a caveat.

The game has a cap on how much Load Experience a digimon can receive from 1 single digimon, this amount is also based on Generation.  
This doesn't limit the total Load Experience but the amount from 1 singular mon.

This means that after a point having Stingmon gain Exp becomes pointless. Below are the exact numbers:

Generation| Load Exp that can be received from 1 mon| Level that can be reached with this Exp  
---|---|---  
In-Training 1| 1000000| 58  
  
In-Training 2  
  
Rookie  
Champion| 800000| 54  
Ultimate  
Mega| 700000| 52  
Mega+| 600000| 50  
  
Now how to get the experience so that Stingmon (and therefore the duped ExVeemon) will reach the Load Exp Cap.

There are 2 methods.

##### Step 2-A: Feedback Loop Method

This method can be done as soon as you gain the ability to go through the evolution loop to duplicate ExVeemon.

Once again the loop is:   
**Stingmon - > JewelBeemon -> TigerVespamon -> Dinobeemon -> Stingmon + ExVeemon**

Produce 10 ExVeemon with the above loop, then feed them ALL to Stingmon.  
This will take about 3 minutes.

Doing this will **DOUBLE** Stingmon's Load Exp.

Repeat this process 7 times.

In total it will take you 21 minutes to reach the Load Exp Cap.

You are now done with the prep if you did this method (Skip 2-B if you did this method).

##### Step 2-B: Olympus 12 Gauntlet Method

This method can only be done once you reach Post Game. So you will likely use it if you are preparing for Mega+ if you didn't use 2-A.

Place Stingmon in your reserve party and defeat the Olympus 12 Gauntlet (in Akihabara Back Alley, Present Day Outer Dungeon).  
It only becomes available from a Post Game Side Quest.

Defeat the Olympus 12, 6 times total and Stingmon will reach the Load Exp Cap.

This will take give or take 18 to 30 minutes depending on how quickly you defeat the Olympus 12.

##### Step 3: ExVeemon Factory Completed

The prep will take about 30 to 40 minutes.

But now that you have reached the Load Exp Cap with Stingmon, all ExVeemon produces will also be at the cap.

1 ExVeemon will level up a digimon to level 50 to 58 (depending on Generation).

You can create 5+ ExVeemon to level any mon to level 99.  
Below are the specifics:

No. of ExVeemon| Time to Produce| Digimon Generation  
---|---|---  
5| 85 seconds| In Training 1  
In Training 2  
Rookie  
6| 102 seconds| Champion  
Ultimate  
7| 119 seconds| Mega  
8| 136 seconds| Mega+  
  
## Digimon Grinding Guides

### Cumulative/Blue Stats Grinding (Including Mega+ Prep)

#### Overview

This method took quite a bit of testing and timing to come up with and it's based on my trial and error and timing.

I am almost certain that with the relase of DLC and updates this may becomes obsolete but if it happens while I am still writing this guide.

I will update it accordingly.

Before we begin I will reitterate how Cummulative stats or blue stats are earned:

##### How to get Blue Stats?

Your white stats are a final total stats based on your Blue stats (if any), the digimon's base stats (the digimon's stat at a specific level under a specific personality) and agent skills you have unlocked.

Generally the formula would be something like this: White Stats = Agent Skills(Base stats + Blue stats).

In case you were wondering it's impossible to see Base stats in the game if you have already unlocked Agent skills affecting stats in every personality, as even the field guide is altered.

When your digimon Trains in the farm or levels up, the game will record the exact number of stats earned from doing so in each stat.

Then when you evolve/devolve the digimon (assuming you have Bond), 10% of the gained stats will be maintained as Cummulative Stats/Blue stats.

If you do not have any usable bond no blue stats are earned. And evolving or devolving will always reset stats of the digimon to level 1 of the digimon it becomes. Hence Blue stats are the only way to maintain the effort of training or leveling a prior form.

There is an exception to this set up.

When fusing two digimon instead of gaining 10% of the stats gained from leveling or training, you gain 5% from both digimon.  
However, if one or both of the fusing digimon ALREADY have blue stats, those existing blue stats will be added up.

Likewise when defusing a digimon their existing blue stats are divided between them.

There is one other way to gain blue stats, this is through Load Enhancements.

When you load a digimon into another, the Loading digimon will gain 50% of the blue stats of the loaded digimon.  
If Veemon loaded an Agumon with 400 ATK Blue stats, then Veemon would get 200 ATK Blue stats.

Also if the loaded digimon has no blue stats but has trained or leveled up to gain stats, 5% of those stats given to the loading digimon.  
If Veemon loaded an Agumon who gained 4000 ATK through training, then Veemon would get 200 ATK Blue stats.

(You'll notice the stat gains are identical, this is because if the Agumon who gained 4000 ATK via training evolved or devolved with bond it would also get 400 Blue stats in its next form).

#### Early Game Method

This method is the fastest for the first half of the game and is useful for early digimon builds when you just want a slight edge when it comes to stats without too much effort.

For this method you need to have arrived in Central Town in the digital world first, but before you return to the real world during mid game.

Go to the Central Tower Entrance, the area you reach by speaking to Pegasusmon in Downtown.

Here go down the ladder. Move between the ladder and the elevator using Digi-Attack to one shot all digimon on the path, including those on the side paths.

If you are having trouble one shotting them, just lower the difficulty to story (remember that level, skill elements, digimon type and whether the attack used is an AOE affects damage of DigiAttacks).

Regardless, as you defeat them convert any digimon that reach 100% conversion rate.

Once all are defeated go up the elevator and then come back.

Do the same thing until you reach the ladder and then return to the elevator.

Repeat this process about 5 times. Each time will be about 1 minute.

Once you have done 5 runs load the converted digimon that have leveled up in your box into the digimon you want to increase blue stats for.

Leave any level 1 digimon alone for now, they won't give blue stats.

Generally this gives around 100 blue stats in every stat every 5 minutes, good value for early game and can make evolution easier.

Continue this process until satisfied.

Be warned you will lose this method when you return to the real world and cannot use it again until end game.

#### Mid Game Method (sorta)

Unfortunately mid game doesn't have as many good easy grinding spots compared Early game.

The best places I have found where you can do a similar loop is the first rooms of Heat and Chill Cosmics.

That said as during mid game you have access to Ultimate digimon it might be better to simply raise many strong digimon at once and load them into each other for faster gains.

#### End Game/Post Game Mega+

This method will require some specific steps and is only applicable once you reach Post Game and are preparing to into Mega+, though aspects can still be used in Endgame for the final battle.

The full method is explained in the below video, it also provides visual representation of the process for your convenience:

Before beginning this method requires you to set up a maxed load duplications loop as explained in this video (please watch step 2 if you have a duplication loop already). Or [read this page](digimon-leveling-guide#leveling-up-any-digimon-in-90-seconds-to-2-minutes).

Once that is set up we can begin the process.

##### Step 0: Creating Blue stat Pooling Rookie Digimon

Create 2 Duplicate ExVeemon (or Ankylomon if using the Angemon Loop), devolve them into any Rookie form. I use Veemon for convenience.

Set their personalities based on the type of digimon you want: Astute for Magic attacker or Zealous for Physical Attacker, healers or debuffer can be either. Don't worry about Personalities skills yet.

Be sure to keep their personalities the same for the best results.

 **Important: Be sure to increase their bond to 10 minimum, but preferred would be 20 to 30(max).**

Place them in your reserve party.

Go to the Olympus 12 Outer Dungeon in Akihabara Present Day.

##### Step 1: Create Fodder

Create 16 Duplicate ExVeemon as fodder. Leave them in the box. (This will take about 4 minutes)

##### Step 2: Gain Exp, Fast via Olympus 12

Set the game to Story difficulty to save time, make sure your party has strong AOE attackers.

Battle the Olympus 12, you can put the fight on Auto, it should take approximately 1 to 2 minutes depending on team optimization.

##### Step 3: Gaining Blue Stats

After winning the battle the 2 Veemon have reached level 70 while the ExVeemon in the box are all level 46.

Use Load Enhancement to load 3 ExVeemon into both Veemon. This will level them up to 98 (and give blue stats).

Then devolve them to In-Training (Tsunomon).

Feed both Tsunomon 5 ExVeemon each so they reach level 99. Evolve them back into Veemon.

##### Step 4: Rinse and Repeat

Now just repeat steps 1 to 3, about 5 times minimum.

After the 5th time you will be have accrued enough Blue stats from this method to create 1 Mega+ ready digimon.

5 loops generally takes 30 minutes total, or a bit more if you take your time.

You CAN continue to do the loop more times if you wish with the 10th time being the point where you don't need to do it anymore.

You can also do a lesser version of the loop where you stop making Fodder and simple fight, devolve, fight, evolve for some quick blue stats gains.

Regardless whether you stop at 5 loops or continue is up to you, I like to stop at 5 because its enough.

##### Step 5: Fusing

Now that we have our 2 Veemon full of Blue stats, we return to the DigiFarm and change one of their personalities to Zealou and the other to Tolerant.

Evolve one into ExVeemon and the other into Stingmon.  
(Veemon -> Tsunomon -> Elecmon -> Kuwagamon -> Okuwamon -> Stingmon)

Fuse the 2 together, this will cause their Blue stats to be added up.

##### Final Step

From here you can take the fused digimon and evolve it to any Mega level or Mega+ digimon you want as the blue stats will allow you to freely evolve them into any non fusions form.

(Note: If the form you want IS a fusion then instead of fusing ExVeemon and Stingmon make them into 2 digimon you want to fuse for your Mega+ digimon such as WarGreymon and MetalGarurumon for Omnimon).

Once you have the digimon you want, just set the personality to what you want, give them the personality skills you want for your set up.  
If your stats still feel unsatisfactory, then you can make up the left over points with training C type, as this will be very small amount and you have plenty of money from fighting the Olympus 12.

In my opinion you need only 3 good digimon for a mega+ run, but you can use this method any number of times you like to create as many near maxed digimon you like.

## Digimon Grinding Guides

### Money Grinding

#### Overview

There are several quick or at least decent ways to make money in digimon time stranger so many in fact I couldn't just list one meta for doing so.

So instead here are all the major methods compiled into one page with the numbers collected for your convenience!

I will not be including DLC...........because why the hell would I? What kind of brain dead guide writer says "Just by the money DLC lol"?

Each method is compared by how much yen can be earned in 1 minute of doing the activity, when they can be done and if there is any prep involved.

Below is a video guide version showing each method in action to help you along if text isn't helpful enough!

Which one you choose may ultimately come down to preference or where in the game you find yourself.

I will also rate each one as shown:

Grade| How good it is  
---|---  
 **S**|  Best  
 **A**|  Fast and Easy  
 **B**|  Good and not to tiresome  
 **C**|  OK, not great  
 **D**|  Really Bad  
  
#### Outer Dungeons

There are 24 Outer dungeons in the game, they have a variety of trials most of which give money and a consumable.

For the sake of this segment I will only be including the 2 Outer Dungeons which give the most amount of money.

Any Outer Dungeon not listed below either take too long to complete or earn lower than 20000 yen per minute.

##### Time Attack Boss Battle 4

Location: Gear Forest: Shade Tree Pier

You can do this method with other Time Attack trials but the money gains will be lower.

For this we will aim to defeat all enemies in the trial by one-shotting them with DigiAttacks.

To do this you need a level 99 digimon (level affects damage), with maxed out ATK or INT depending on the skills.  
Lastly their personality should be Sly. You will need the Sly Fundraising Agent Skill.

The reason for this specific set up is because this will let us collect money from the defeated digimon and increasing those earnings via Sly Fundraising.

Be sure the Sly Digimon is the only digimon in active party so they kill all the enemies.

If you have Millionaire USB DLC you can use it here too but I will assume you don't. If you have trouble one shotting digimon you can set the difficulty down to Story.

Begin a run and one shot all enemies EXCEPT Kuwagamon who don't give any money or exp.

In total you will earn yen from the below enemies:

  1. Kabuterimon: 614 x 9 = 5526
  2. Okuwamon: 1286 x 2 = 2572
  3. MegaKabuterimon: 1286 x 2 = 2572



TyrantKabuterimon will give 2649 yen on defeat.

Thus our total from enemies is 13319.

For completing the trial we receive 20000 yen and a Full Revive Spray we can sell for 4000 yen.

Thus our total earnings from one run is: 37,319. The total time needed is 40 seconds.

Using ratios to calculate for one minute is 55,978 yen (rounded down).

Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 55,978  
---|---  
Pros| A Single Run is very quick,  
Very little prep, except for Agent Skill and Personality  
Lowering difficulty removes need for high stat digimon  
Cons| Not possible with other Time Attacks,   
can only be accessed in the 2nd half of the game.  
Verdict|  **B+**  
  
##### Speed is Victory 4

Speed is Victory is often regarded as one of the worst and most annoying trials due to the controls and the difficulty in clearing races without hitting anything.

However, it also ends up being a great way to grind money.

During the race against MagnaGarurumon, there are Pink Crates, some of these have Sapphire inside.

It is possible to collect these by hitting the crates. If you win the race you receive them in addition to the reward.

You can generally obtain between 4 and 8 during one race. A race takes 1 minute 40 seconds at most.

Each Sapphire can be sold for 10000 yen. We also pay 5000 for the ticket.

The main reward is 20000 yen + Full Revive which can be sold for 4000.

Thus our total earnings are 19000 + (Sapphires found x 10000).  
Minimum this can be 59000 yen and max 99000 yen, in 1 minute 40 seconds.

Using ratios this gives us: 35,400 minimum to 59,400 yen maximum per minute.

Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 35,400 to 59,400   
(Depends on number of Sapphires collected)  
---|---  
Pros| No digimon battle prep.  
Races are quick  
Cons| It is a hassle trying to win the race and collect Sapphire  
Not possible with other Races,   
can only be accessed in the 2nd half of the game.  
Verdict|  **C to B+  
(Depends on person's tolerance for this mini game)**  
  
#### Selling Skill Disks

This method can be done once you have access to Mega level, there is a bit of prep necessary but it's a very quick set up.

It is recommended you have as many nodes in the Wisdom Agent Skill Tree, but you want as many of the 4 Digivolution of Wisdom nodes.

Create a Lilymon with Strategic Personality to allow her to instantly evolve to Ceresmon even at level 1 with the above Agent Skills.

Make sure Ceresmon has used up all her Attachment skill slots so all skills go to the inventory.

The next requirement is you need to have created an ExVeemon duplication loop, with maxed out Load Exp. This is explained in the below video, up till step 2:

Once you have that set up. We can begin.

Evolve Lilymon to Ceresmon. Create 4 ExVeemon.

Feed 1 to Ceresmon. This SHOULD level her up to level 52 and she will give the following Skill Disk:

  1. Thorn Strike III
  2. X Revive
  3. X Aura
  4. Restore



X Aura and X Revive are the most expensive skill disks in the game.

Then devolve her back to Lilymon and then back to Ceresmon, feed her another ExVeemon.

Repeat this process until you have fed her all the currently created ExVeemon.

Then sell your Skill Disks.

The total earned from selling them is 71200 yen, in 2 minutes. Thus you would be earning 35600 yen per minute.

Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 35600  
---|---  
Pros| No battles needed,  
Quick and Easy to do.  
Cons| Boring and requires setting up Load Exp maxed ExVeemon loop  
Requires Rank 7 Agent Rank and need to be near the end of the 2nd half of the game.  
Verdict|  **C**  
  
#### Magnamon Duplication/Return to Data Method

I dislike this method because of how inefficient it is but I'll include it anyway, just to prove how bad it is.

You need to obtain the Digiegg of Miracles, and have a Rank 7 Agent Rank.

The method is simple after creating an ExVeemon Duplication loop, Step 1 in below video:

Then create 4 ExVeemon and devolve them to Veemon and then evolve them into Magnamon.

With that done, Return all of them to data to net 40000 yen.

This process take 2 minutes approximately, 1 minute to make 4 ExVeemon and 1 more minute to change them to Magnamon and return them to data.

Thus the total earned per minute is 20000 yen.

Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 20000  
---|---  
Pros| No battles needed  
Cons| Requires Digiegg of Miracles and Rank 7 Agent Rank  
Tedious, need to set up ExVeemon loop  
Inferior to other methods  
Verdict|  **D-**  
  
#### Olympus 12 Gauntlet

A lot of players consider this to be the fastest method to earn money. They are right for the most part.

Once you reach post game you will unlock a bonus outer dungeon in Akihabara (Present Day) where you get to fight all members of the Olympus 12 at once.

The fight itself takes generally 3 to 5 minutes normally. Even when playing optimally and one shotting them, they still get their own turns so the time taken is longer than expected.

If the difficulty is lowered to Story it can be brought down to 1 minute. With a full party of AOE attacks. It also allows you to fight using Auto and without needing to make any input.  
However, many players aren't too keen on lowering difficulty for this fight when grinding but it's a matter of preference.

If you defeat them you earn: 309600 yen.

However, if all your active party members who defeated one of them has a Sly Personality and you have the Sly Fundraising Agent Skill.

You can increase your earnings to: 367392 yen in 1 or 3 minutes (assuming efficiency of party).

The overall efficiency of this method comes down to whether your willing to lower the difficulty to speed things up.

Difficulty| Story| Hard  
---|---|---  
Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 367392| 122,464  
Pros| Generally pretty fast, the fastest if done on Story.  
Can be farmed for money while doing other farming with the Olympus 12  
Cons| Fastest speed requires lowering the difficulty  
Requires a pretty strong party to begin with if not lowering difficulty  
Post Game, you'll mainly be using when preparing for NG+ and is hence  
useless to those who don't want to go into NG+  
Verdict|  **A to S+**  
  
#### Royal Knights Grinding

Much like the Olympus 12 this involves fighting a boss and earning a lot of money.

Unlike the Olympus 12 all the Royal knight fights are one on one in the digital world on top of Central Tower (Present).

The Royal Knights are all relatively easier to defeat than the Olympus 12 Gauntlet. They are still quite strong but for this method, we will be aiming to 1 shot at least one of them.

While dangerous they can be easily cheesed by defeating them before they even get a turn.

We will use the following set up:

  1. Create a Beelzemon BM, with maxed Attack Stat and high Speed Stat (about 6000 should do it).
  2. His personality should be Sly, and you should have Sly Fundraising Agent Skill. Remove any Attachment Skills.
  3. Equip him with: Blue Steel Data Fragment and Sky God's Thunderbolt (the 2nd one is optional anything that increases crit rate is fine)
  4. For this Personality Skill equip Planning Ahead.
  5. Your other 2 party members can be any digimon slower than Beelzemon BM, both should have Cheer as their personality skill.



Put on Auto Battle and fight Dynasmon. He is a Data type and has the lowest defense among his peers.

You will one shot him and then fight ends in less than 5 seconds. This nets us about 36096 yen per fight.

In 1 minute you can fight him 7 times, earning us 252,672 per minute.

The above information is for fighting the Royal knights on Hard, but if you are willing to lower the difficulty then you can one shot any of them with any set up (just make sure the digimon that kills them is Sly).

Difficulty| Story| Hard  
---|---|---  
Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 252672  
Pros| VERY FAST!  
Can be done with no set up.   
Aside from making a digimon Sly|   
Cons|  | Requires some set up.  
End Game, must complete all Royal Knight side quests first  
Verdict|  **S**  
  
#### Digi-Jewel Method

The full method is posted [here](valuable-grinding-digi-jewel-grinding).

To save time as I have covered this method on that page, here is the video guide for it:

This method allows us to collect a Digi-Jewel from the same location over and over using save and loading to respawn the item.

 **WATCH THE VIDEO OR GO TO THE LINKED PAGE FOR THE FULL METHOD THERE ARE SPECIFIC STEPS FOR THIS EXPLOIT!!**

Depending on if you can find 2 spawn points or just 1, the gains vary.

Each DigiJewel sells for 50000.

With 1 spawn point 3 Digi-Jewels can be obtained per minute, thus earning 150000 yen per minute.  
With 2 spawn points 6 can be collected per minute, thus earning 300000 yen per minute.

Spawn Points| 1| 2  
---|---|---  
Earnings In 1 Minute (Yen)| 150000| 300000  
Pros| Very fast, can be done without battle  
Can be done at any point in the game not just end game.  
You only need to find 1 Digi-Jewel in one area in the digital world  
Can be done with Valuables OTHER than Digi-Jewels  
Cons| This is an exploit, so it may get patched.  
Verdict|  **A to S+**  
  
## Digimon Grinding Guides

### Valuable Grinding (Digi-Jewel Grinding)

#### About Valuables

When checking Material collection spots in the game, each area has a different pool of items.   
(In this case area refers to a single "room").

Rarely you will get a gemstone be it Aquamarine, Emerald or the ever elusive Digi-Jewel.

These for the most part have no purpose except being sold for some extra money. They occur randomly but there are ways to grind them.

![](/ffaq/40/327/)

Every room tends to give one specifc gemstone, but the DigiJewel is obtainable from ANY material collection spot in the digital world.  
However the drop rate of Digi-Jewels is very low in most areas.

While the below grinding method can be used for any Valuable we will focus on Digi-Jewels for convenience and because they are the most valuable.

(You also need to trade 3 for a Digi Egg of Miracles but you won't need it till Agent Rank 7).

#### How to Grind Digi-Jewels

Above is a video example of this method.

First we need a location where we can easily grind out Digi-Jewels, so a place with a lot of possible collection points.

The best location is the Dark Zone: Dark Castle (not Dark Garden).

Enter the area via the garden and check all the Material collection spots until you find a Digi-Jewel, this won't take too long usually around 10 minutes at worst 30.

The item pool for this area is:

Item/Material  
---  
Panic Recovery  
Texture   
(1 of 2 possible textures)  
Int Boost  
Emerald  
Digi-Jewel  
  
Once you find a Digi-Jewel, go to another collection point, collect the item and save the game.  
 **Note: You can just open the menu and exit out to trigger and Auto Save, it's faster than a normal save.**

Load your data and return to where you found the Digi-Jewel. The collection spot will respawn and have the exact same item.

This process can be looped infinitely.

Ideally if you find 2 or more spots with a Digi-Jewel you can cycle between them.

Because Digi Jewels sell for 50000 yen, and with this method you can collect 1 every 10 to 30 seconds you can make over 500000 in 5 minutes or less.

This makes it by and far the fastest method to earn money (and why it is not in the main Money Grinding list).

##### Disclaimer:

This method CAN be used anywhere in the digital world if you can find a Digi-Jewel or any Valuable like Aquamarine. But the later in the game the better the valuables.

## Outer Dungeons Guide

#### About Outer Dungeons

Outer Dungeons are a side activity or challenge you can participate in freely when finding them in various locations throughout the digital world and human world.

Essentially these are either mini games or battle challenges of varying type and difficulty,

They appear in the world as glowing red diamonds, sometimes visible easily other times hidden by destroyable objects (reveal them with Digi-Attacks.) Once you find a dungeon you do not need to do them immediately as once found it is registered and can be played from the Theater.

![](/ffaq/40/872/)

While in Outer Dungeons and doing the challenges you will be under restrictions, you won't be able to use the menu to change your party, or access the majority of settings. You also cannot use items in battle or outside. Auto Regen is also prevented.

Every challenge has 3 rewards. The first is the free digimon you obtain upon completing the challenge once, these will appear on the   
giant keychain interacting with it will allow you to add the digimon to your box.

The 2nd prize are the completion rewards which you can get multiple times, usually money and consumables, these depend on the  
difficulty/number of the Outer Dungeon as below:

Outer Dungeon  
Number| 1| 2| 3| 4  
---|---|---|---|---  
Money| 5000| 10000| 15000| 20000  
Consumable| Medical Spray| Medical Spray DX| Revival Capsule DX| Full Revival Spray  
Friendship| Friendship S  
Challenge Reward   
(one time only)| Talent Awakening   
Booster x1| Talent Awakening  
Booster x2| Talent Awakening  
Booster x3| Talent Awakening  
Booster x5  
  
![](/ffaq/40/873/)

The other prize involves the purple crystal next to the keychain, these can only be opened to receive Talent Awakening Boosters and a message from the creator of the challenge (lore) after beating an additional challenge during the minigame.

 **Please note: To get the trophy/steam achievement you ONLY need to complete every Outer Dungeon once and do not need to  
complete the Purple Crystal Challenge.**

#### Time Attack Boss Battle

These challenges are pretty simple. Reach the boss at the end of the dungeon and defeat the boss within the time limit.

In addition to the boss the arena is filled with a variety of enemies, you can defeat them or ignore them,   
as long as you reach the boss on time.

The arena is also home to Green Outer Dungeon chests, these are one time chests similar to those found out in the world.

In addition, there may be purple crystal chests, these always contain Enhancement Chips which you can use to increase digimon stats.  
These purple chests respawn either after time passes OUTSIDE the dungeon or if you visit another Outer Dungeon and return.   
A low key way to grind chips but not particularly fast.

The Purple Crystal Challenge for these dungeons requires you to defeat a certain number of enemies with a Digi-Attack.

 **Tip: To easily one shot digimon with DigiAttacks, keep in mind the type of digimon in your party and reserve party as well as their  
** **highest power attacks, keep in mind that AOE attacks also help to defeat multiple digimon in an encounter. The Higher your digimon's Level the more easy it is to one shot them. If you want to skip the effort of needing to do this just lower the difficulty and one shot the necessary digimon and then return the difficulty to normal when done.**

Location| Rewards  
---|---  
![](/ffaq/40/877/)| Digimon Reward|  **Reppamon**  
Location| Akihabara: A Certain Back Alley  
Challenge| Defeat 10 enemies with Digi-Attack  
Walkthrough|  **[Time Attack 1](chapter-4-monica-simmons-and-outer-dungeons#time-attack-boss-battle-1)**  
![](/ffaq/40/875/)|  Digimon Reward|  **Ikkakumon**  
Location| Abyss Beach  
Challenge| Defeat 10 enemies with Digi-Attack  
Walkthrough|  **[Time Attack 2](chapter-8-song-of-the-abyss#time-attack-boss-battle-2)**  
![](/ffaq/40/876/)|  Digimon Reward|  **MetalTyrannomon**  
Location| Shinjuku: East Shopping District  
Challenge| Defeat 10 enemies with Digi-Attack  
Walkthrough|  **[Time Attack 3](chapter-17-foolish-humans#time-attack-boss-battle-3-and-4)**  
![](/ffaq/40/874/)|  Digimon Reward|  **TyrantKabuterimon**  
Location| Gear Forest: Shade Tree Pier  
Challenge| Defeat 10 enemies with Digi-Attack  
Walkthrough|  **[Time Attack 4](chapter-17-foolish-humans#time-attack-boss-battle-3-and-4)**  
  
#### Survive to the End

This challenge is pretty simple. The only requirement is for you to run the timer out and not be defeated by any enemies in the small arena as they constantly spawn in.

This is one of the easier challenges overall as there is nothing to worry about other than defeating enemies and not letting yourself   
be caught in the arena.

The Purple Crystal Challenge for these is always to survive until the end and never be hit by an enemy premptive attack (let them start a fight with an advantage).

 **Tip: The easiest way to avoid this is to avoid enemies and Digi-Attack any that get close to force a battle. You can also force a battle to buy you time while you escape from a sticky situation.  
To make it even easier if your level and digimon are strong enough you can one shot enemies with Digi-Attacks as they spawn in to avoid any trouble at all making the challenge much easier.   
Just be sure you have the means to win the battles if you end up fighting. Lowering the difficulty will make this a lot easier.**

Location| Rewards  
---|---  
![](/ffaq/40/881/)| Digimon Reward|  **Raremon**  
Location| Shinjuku: A Certain Back Alley  
Challenge| Survive until the end without receiving any  
preemptive attacks from the enemy  
Walkthrough|  **[Survive 1](chapter-4-monica-simmons-and-outer-dungeons#survive-to-the-end-1)**

#### Protect The Giant DigiMeat

This is another simple challenge. The arena is a large stretch of road where enemy digimon will run across their end trying to reach the goal  
on your side to reach the Digi Meat. Your goal is to defeat them before they reach the meat.

Unlike normal, digi-attacking enemies does not kill enemies and will instead lower their HP or kill them if enough damage is dealt.

You will be running around the field using digi-attack to hit enemies and defeat them before they reach their target.

Different digimon have different speeds and some prioritize attacking you, while others make a bee line for the meat.

Remember you can run by holding the circle (or Cancel on whichever platform) button and to catch up to any you miss.

It is recommended you keep the map open so you can see where the enemy digimon are.

Some digimon can also fly high up making them impossible to hit unless they are about to fly or are coming down.

If an enemy touches you, a battle is triggered like normal.

Generally it is recommended to stay close to the enemy side and defeat enemies as they come out as fast as possible without missing any.

In total you need to defeat 30 digimon.

The challenge objective is to defeat all enemies with only DigiAttack and not being forced into battles.

 **Tip: As with other challenges DigiAttack Strength comes down to level, skill element and digimon type so making  
note of the enemy types and having a party that has the type advantage while also being high level generally helps.   
Lowering difficulty will also make this easier.**

Location| Rewards  
---|---  
![](/ffaq/40/882/)| Digimon Reward|  **Dinohyumon**  
Location| Underground Passage  
The place where you first   
enter the digital world  
Challenge| Defeat all enemies with DigiAttack  
Walkthrough|  **[DigiMeat 2](chapter-6-welcome-to-the-digital-world#protect-the-giant-meat-2)**  
![](/ffaq/40/885/)|  Digimon Reward|  **Wizardmon**  
Location| Gear Forest Village  
Challenge| Defeat all enemies with DigiAttack  
Walkthrough|  **[DigiMeat 1](chapter-9-the-gear-forest#protect-the-giant-digimeat-1)**

#### Speed is Victory

This is a pure minigame and a point of much contention for folks.

Unlike other challenges in order to participate you first need a Vade Ticket bought from the Vademon to the right of the keychain.

The game is a simple race against a pixel digimon while you right a Peckmon.

The controls are very simple, you do not control speed or direction the Peckmon faces, instead you use the left stick to control whether you  
turn and which direction, you do not control the degree of the turn and you remain going in a straight line except when turning corners.

Obstacles and digimon will get in the way and slow you down temporarily where as digimeat will give you a speed boost.

Materials can be obtained from crates and rare sellable ones from pink crates.

The controls take some time to practice and get used to, but it is doable to win the races with limited difficulty.

The challenge for these is to beat each race while hitting less than 4 (3 minimum) obstacles and digimon, this was not the case originally and was changed in an update at some point (originally it was without hitting any).

The races have also been made easier than they were at launch.

 **Tip: Generally speaking sometimes it is best not to boost, and you can predict when moving digimon will be in a certain position based on how quickly you reach a certain point with or without boosts. Practice makes perfect.**

Challenge| Avoid hitting more than 3 obstacles/enemies

#### Fight the Ultimate Digimon

These involve fighting custom made digimon with higher than normal stats. To put it simply consider them to be player developed digimon with higher stats than wild digimon, as if they were put through training and have higher stats.

Each of these challenges requires you to pick between 2 digimon to fight, defeat it, pick from 2 more digimon and then do it one more time.

You will fight 3 digimon, of increasing difficulty. The overall difficulty depends on the route you choose as some digimon at the end of are stronger than the others or at a higher stage.

In total each of these has 14 digimon to fight each, with 52 different digimon across each of these Outer Dungeons.

For every digimon you defeat you receive trophies representing that you had defeated that digimon. These are Marks of Mastery.

Unlike the challenges in other Outer Dungeons the challenge for these is to collect a certain number of Marks of Mastery.   
Duplicate trophies do not count so you need an X number of unique trophies. As each challenge has only 14 digimon you will need to fight  
several or all digimon across several of these Outer dungeons to complete the Purple Crystal Challenge.

This is the simplest challenge and the only way to make it easier is to have stronger digimon.

 **It should be noted that changing difficulty whether increasing or decreasing only affect the digimon's attack stats  
not defensive stats.**

---|---

the barrels (does not appear until 2nd visit)

## Jogmon Ultimate Card Game

#### About Jogmon

This is a playable card game you can play as you explore the human and digital worlds.

You gain access to the game shortly after the initial parts of the game and can then challenge opponents with the initial cards you receive.

Unlike most mini games in RPGs this is a complete side activity and one you do not need to complete for any achievement or in game goal, the only things you need to do is optain a specific card and win a single game.

Aside from this certain quests at the end game will also require winning a few games but they are very few.

All this is to say you do not need to play the mini game much or if at all even if you just want to complete the card collection, more on that below.

#### How to play Jogmon

You can challenge any opponent with the card battle icon over their heads. Every opponent you find can be challenged repeatedly even after being defeated.

Unlike standard card games there are no deck building aspects to Jogmon. The cards you use are picked from your current collection of cards.

At the start of the game both players draw 5 cards from their collections. These make up their hands and both can see the others' hands.

![](/ffaq/40/866/)

Players choose digimon from their hand to put into battle. Once selected, the 2 digimon will have a simulated battle Tamagotchi style.

![](/ffaq/40/867/)

The victor is determined by the type and number of the card. As with normal battles the digimon type advantages come into play here, Virus is strong against Data, Data is stronger than Vaccine and Vaccine is stronger than Virus. The other types are neutral.

![](/ffaq/40/363/)

Beyond this the number on the top left of the cards determines their "strength" this usually relates to their generation (evolution stage), this is also represented by the card's color. As represented in the below table:

Number| Color| Generation  
---|---|---  
1| Red| In Training 1  
2| Green| In Training 2  
3| Blue| Rookie  
4| Purple| Champion  
(Armor and Spirit included)  
5| Silver| Ultimate  
(Spirit included)  
6| Gold| Mega  
(Armor and Spirit included)  
7| Diamond| Mega+  
  
When two digimon are selected the winner is decided based on which has the highest number (if they aren't the same) and which has the type advantageous (if any), however there is also a degree of randomness which becomes more pronounced the more you play. At times a digimon with a disadvantage or a lower strength will win.

Sometimes when defeated a digimon can get a second wind and enter brave and cause a 2nd battle (once).

Regardless one digimon will be defeated and the controlling player will win that round.

There are 5 rounds in total in 1 match, the winner is the one who wins the most matches.

When you win or lose the match you can gain new cards that were used in the match by your opponent that you do not currently own. The number you can get is based on the number of rounds you won.

Winning all 5 rounds earns you 3 cards, winning 4 or 3 earns you 2 cards and winning 1 gets you just 1 card.

For more details scroll down to the In-Depth Breakdown.

![](/ffaq/40/868/)

##### Card Combination and Creating new Cards

There is an additional mechanic in these card battles which can be used to create new cards beyond the 5 you start with, both players can make use of this if the conditions are correct.

If at the start of your turn your hand has a specific combination of cards (and no extras) which have SOMETHING in common, these cards can be used to create a new "Extra Card". The created card is based on the cards used to create it.

![](/ffaq/40/869/)

There are 2 types of combinations, standard combinations (2x Rookies, 2x In-Training, 2x Virus, 2x Vaccine etc) essentially these are cards with matching number or types, and unique combinations. Unique combinations can be a combination of 2 or more related cards and even up to 5 cards to create a new card.

Standard combinations may produce randomized results and can be repeated for varying/worse results, but unique combinations are one time uses.

The below screenshot shows the combination used to create Kimeramon using 5 cards.

![](/ffaq/40/870/)

Any cards created with extra card summon (that you don't already own) will be added to prize pot at the end of the match, to add the extra cards to your deck you must use the extra card during a round and then select it as a prize after the end.

#### In-Depth Breakdown: Card Combinations and Hands

Despite popular belief the hands you AND your opponent draw in battle are NOT random at least not entirely.

In regards to your opponent they often will have a pool of specific hands (or cards) they will get, depending on how many cards they have in their pool they have a higher variety of hands. This is to ensure the player can obtain a variety of cards when facing them.

Your own hand is determined by 2 conditions:

The first is your opponent, you may notice if you play the game a lot that certain cards in your early game collection will not appear against certain opponents. This is because your hand is restricted based on your current collection and the cards your opponent has. After all it would be a bit unfair if you had end game cards (via NG+) and your opponent had only low stage cards. Thus your cards are limited to a specific pool. This applies to all opponents until near the end game where the pool expands as near the end game

The 2nd condition is your own collection and what cards you currently own.

Based on the above 2 conditions the game will narrow down which cards you are allowed to use for the given opponent and then randomly select one of the possible Unique Extra cards you can create with your current collection. It will then place all the cards for that combination in your hand. If the combination is less than 5 cards it will add "Filler" cards to round out the number.

Filler cards will also tend towards being low strength or high strength or armor or variable (spirit) cards as they have few or no combinations.

Also the game will tend to prioritize Extra Card summons for cards you DO NOT have, over those you do assuming you have the necessary cards.

 **In Summary: The cards in your hand are not randomly drawn, there is always an extra summon card combination, you need to figure out which cards are filler and remove them by playing them to trigger the summon.**

#### Card List

In total there are 451 cards. Each card corresponds to a digimon in your field guide and matches its ID number. The exception to this is MagnaGarurumon (Detached) which does not have a card, instead the 451st card is a digimon you cannot use in battle and can only be obtained after completing a certain question line (more on that later).

Below is a list of all cards (identical to field guide).

#### Completing your Collection

There are 4 ways to add cards to your collection:

  1. Win cards from your opponents
  2. Create Extra Summon cards and then receive the card from the prize pool.
  3. Buy them from Card Shops
  4. Receive them as drops from enemy digimon (Certain story/optional boss digimon have a 100% chance of dropping a card upon defeat)



The first 2 methods have already been explained, so we'll go over the latter 2.

##### Your Starting Cards:

The following cards are given to you automatically when you unlock the card (as such they are not sold in shops and do not need to be obtained by other means:

No.| Digimon| Generation| Strength  
---|---|---|---  
010|  **Tanemon**|  In Training 2|  **2**  
015|  **Nyaromon**|  In Training 2|  **2**  
021|  **Agumon**|  Rookie|  **3**  
057|  **Lunamon**|  Rookie|  **3**  
202|  **ExTyrannomon**|  Ultimate|  **5**  
289|  **Vademon**|  Ultimate|  **5**  
  
##### Shops and Shop Locations

In every location in the digital world you will find a Dracmon Card Shop, usually he appears in the town areas or near Locomon as shown below. In the human world there is a single card shop in the Akihabara Back Alley. Keep in mind that the cards in the shop will be updated as you proceed through the story. Shops in the same area will have the same stock regardless of time period they are visited in.

![](/ffaq/40/871/)

Aside from GoldNumemon appearing in 2 shops all shops have unique cards not found in the other shops (but which can be aquired by playing against opponents in the area).

Below is a list of cards you can obtain from each shop, blank spaces are cards which are not in shops and must be obtained through other ways (or are already obtained via starter deck). If a card is not in the shop in your game advance the story and they will appear later, by end game all cards will be available as shown in the below list:

Blank Card  
  
*Ukkomon is obtained after completing the "The Pinnacle of the Card World" Quest in the end game and receiving the Blank Card. Take it to any of the card shops and pay 5000 yen to create the card.

##### Card's obtained by defeating enemies

All digimon you face in battle have a chance to drop cards of themseves. The drop rate is improved the higher the bond of the digimon who defeats them. The drop rates have also been improved after updates in November 2025.

Several cards are obtained as guaranteed drops from enemy bosses or sub bosses during story battles these are listed below:

#### Unique Combinations

Below is a list of known combinations needed to create specific Extra cards, this list is not complete and simply what i could gather off the internet, this is more a bonus as you DEFINITELY do not need to these to obtain every card. It's just here for crazy people (like me) who like playing the card game:

## Walkthrough

## Walkthrough

### Starting the game

#### Choosing the difficulty

Before you start you will be asked to choose the difficulty of the game, the 2 hardest modes Mega and Mega+ are unavailable on a first run of the game.

For now here are the difficulties available to you:

Difficulty| Description  
---|---  
Story| Story focused, you will be able to retry fights while invincible mode, basically easy mode  
Balanced| Combat is balanced not super easy, but not super hard  
Hard| Combat requires awareness of party composition and digimon training,  
you have to engage properly with the mechanics to succeed.  
  
For the sake of this guide on a first run I will be going with Hard.

#### Choosing your protagonist

You will then be made to choose between the 2 protagonists. The boy Dan Yuki, or the girl Kanon Yuki.   
They are not the same person and which ever you dont choose will play a supporting role during the story, it won't diverge the story however.

You can also change their names.

You can also change the character and the name at any time you want.

The game will then proceed through some scenes.

Note: For those who want to play in japanese your gonna have to wait a bit to change the language unfortunately.

In any case sit back and watch the scenes as Mirei Mikagura (a returning character from prior story games, also she is younger for some reason this time.)

## Walkthrough

### Prologue: Agent of Adamas, The Mysterious Girl and Digimon

#### Introduction

Watch the opening scenes during the discussion with Operator you can choose the bottom two choices to learn more about the setting and then advance by selecting the top option.

After the scenes, you have access to the System menu from Options/Start/+.

You can go into the options menu to change language, difficulty, appearance and name of your character.

Also you can access music settings to change the background music for specific situations and set them to your liking if you bought the music related DLC.

You can also use your digivice but it doesn't have any proper functions at the moment.

We're currently in Higashi Shinjuku: Vision Square. You can speak to the NPCs in the are before going to the objective using the map.

##### Koshu Kaido Intersection and Seibu Shinjuku: Overbridge Intersection

When you reach the objective you will receive a message from Operator via Digiline and can read some new tutorials, the system section also includes some lore on the setting.

Continue to the next area following the objective and then straight to the next area for a scene.

##### Wall of Hope

After the scene go up and speak to the man in front of you.

Make your way through the crowd until a scene plays.

After that make your way to the exit underground.

#### Forgotten Rails

During the scenes you will find digimon wandering around and Operator will present you the choice of your first digimon.

For those new to this game don' worry too much about this choice, you will have a chance to obtain any of these digimon later and likely oobtain stronger versions of them at that point. So just pick whichever you like.

I went with Demi Devimon.

![](/ffaq/40/2/)

Then approach any digimon and a battle will begin.

##### The First Battle

The game will explain the basic digimon attribute triangle, this is common knowledge for digimon fans but for those out of the know consider it a rock paper scissors relationship of which type is effective against what.

From here you can get the basic battle screen as shown below:

![](/ffaq/40/3/)

These commands are standard for anyone used to turn based combat. Keep the type advantages in mind too when fighting. 

You can also analyze digimon to see their stats or weaknesses if needed.

After the battle all battle tutorials will unlock so give them a read to familiarize yourself with the mechanics.

In addition to Attributes there are elements with each digimon having one of 11 possible elements (Fire, water, earth etc).

They also weak to specific elements just like they are weak to certain attributes. 

This is very similar to SMT and persona so if you're familiar with those this should be easy to understand.

The Traits of a digimon also effect how much damage they do to each other.

There are also a two types of attacks in the game, physical and magic, some digimon are resistant to one (or both types).

Lastly below is a quick break down of stats for your mons:

Stat| Description  
---|---  
ATK| Affects Physical damage  
DEF| Affects how much Physical damage from enemies is reduced  
INT| Affects Magic damage  
SPI| Affects how much Magic damage from enemies is reduced  
SPD| Affects Turn Order  
CRT Rate| Affects the Critical damage rate (criticals deal 1.3x more damage)  
  
You also may have noticed your digimon has a level limit, this is it's "Talent". Once your digimon can digivolve it will increase the level limit.  
And when it de-evolves it will retain the new level limit.

Another somewhat hidden stat is Bond, the higher this value the more cummulative stats the digimon retains between evolutions and de-evolutions, more on that later.

The digimon in this game can also have equipment, and can use skill disks to equip generic skills. Attachment skills are learnt naturally as they level up and can be equipped or unequipped.

Like in cyber sleuth digimon have personality types, which affect stat growth.

This time there are four types:

  1. Valor: ATK increases easily
  2. Amicability: DEF increases easily
  3. Philanthropy: SPI increases easily
  4. Wisdom: INT increases easily



The above are broken down into 4 sub types each creating a 4x4 grid for additional personalities.

Personality all effects certain skills and mechanics. Personality can be changed a little based on interactions with your digimon, you can talk to your mon on the field and the choices given help shift the personality to a different type after enough time and interactions.

You can now also check the field guide for info on digimon you have seen and acquired (note: the 2 digimon you didn't select are automatically added).

The information on digimon you don't have is incomplete but the digimon you do have has very detailed info.

You can even check their stats at any level from 1 to 99.

Once you have dealt with Gotsumon, Impmon and Monodramon proceed and Operator will inform you about DigiAttacks. This is basically a symbol attack on the field letting you get the jump on enemies and destroy objects.

Digiattacks let you finish off enemies without getting into a fight if you are stronger than them, test it out on the Koromon and Impmon then advance.

Before the next objective is a treasure chest it contains 2 Sp Capsules, items you can use to recover SP for your digimon.

If and when your digimon levels up to level 2 they will likely learn an attachment skill you can equip or unequip as you want (HP and SP also recover)

![](/ffaq/40/4/)

Make your way further in after the above image and you'll see some junk with a symbol on it. This will let you collect Gray Texture, a material we'll need later.

![](/ffaq/40/5/)

Advance a little further in and near a Gotsumon is another chest containing, an HP attachment I. You can equip this to your mon right away via the setup menu. 

![](/ffaq/40/6/)

Continue on the path and on the way after a Monodramon is another cart with items with more Grey Texture.

![](/ffaq/40/7/)

Nothing else here so continue to the exit while fighting through any digimon.

#### Forgotten Rails 2

Make your way to the objective for a scene. Then use the DigiAttack to clear the rubble.

Go further in and a scene will play.

YOU WILL THEN BE CHASED BY METAL GREYMON (BLUE), hold the stick downwards to reach the end of the tunnel (it's a bit finicky as if you start too late you get trampled and have to restart).

After that point we will have scenes, answer the dialogue however you like.

Then speak to the mystery girl, check the door and then use a DigiAttack to open it.

Once through the door, look left to find another cart with, Gravel Dataset.

![](/ffaq/40/9/)

Then proceed west on the map to find another one near the end of the room with, Rock Model Fragment.

![](/ffaq/40/10/)

Then go north to another part of the train track and in the north west end of the area in a hole in the wall is a chest containing, an Icicle Edge I Skill Disk. Go ahead and equip this for now.

![](/ffaq/40/11/)

Now return the room and proceed, you will be informed about Regeneration Mode, your digimon will recover in a safe area while standing still.

Continue until you reach some more tracks, go north west and stop when you find a cart, it contains 2 more Grey Texture.

![](/ffaq/40/12/)

Then directly in front, at the end of tracks near the rubble ahead is another chest, this one has 3 HP Capsules.

![](/ffaq/40/13/)

From here go to the next area.

#### Giant Pit

Go to the objective for a scene and you will have your first boss fight.

You will be fighting Metal Greymon (Blue) alongside Omnimon. Omnimon is a guest so he won't be directly controlled.

This fight is more or less scripted as Omnimon will be doing most of the damage. You just have to focus on staying alive.

![](/ffaq/40/14/)

After the fight and scenes climb up the rubble to the next area.

#### Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building: Entrance

Well this place has seen better days.

At this point Operator will explain how scanning digimon and scan rate works for acquiring new mons.

In essence everytime you defeat a digimon the scan rate increases and at 100% you can create that digimon, but at 200% you can creat the digimon with higher Max HP and Talent (higher max level).

You are automatically given Koromon 200% Scan rate and can create one. The higher ranked a digimon the longer it takes to fully scan.

You will also unlock Load Enhancements which you can use to sacrifice one digimon to another and increase the latter's Exp.

I don't recommend doing this yet, for now just add Koromon to your battle roster.

Go slightly north and turn east all the way to find a chest in the corner, it contains, 3 HP Capsules.

![](/ffaq/40/15/)

Enter the building, from here on I will catalogue each digimon you encounter in a given area as a type of checklist for you.

Note: There is a Chaosdramon in here and it's not something you can sensibly defeat now.

Digimon Found  
---  
Gazimon| Tentomon| Botamon  
Gotsumon| Toyagumon| Impmon  
Kotemon| Monodramon|   
Punimon| Chaosdramon  
(Impossible to defeat)|   
Dracomon| Wanyamon|   
  
Once inside the building first let's deal with the area east of the entrance as it leads to a dead end.

Turn to the north immediately to find a cart containing an SP Capsule.

![](/ffaq/40/22/)'

Continue east to see an Impmon near it is another cart containing, Gravel Data Sets.

![](/ffaq/40/23/)

Continue east again, to the south east end of the building to find another cart (near Kotemon) containing, Rock Model Fragment.

![](/ffaq/40/24/)

Then proceed north to find a chest with Plant Guard, this is an equipment item which reduces damage from plant type enemies.

![](/ffaq/40/25/)

Now return to the entrance and take the other path, go to the south west end of the building (where there is a Dracomon) and you will find a chest in the corner containing, Wolkenpalm I.

![](/ffaq/40/16/)

Then directly north is a cart with, Brown Texture.

![](/ffaq/40/17/)

There are 3 carts near Chaosdramon, one to the north wall, which has Grey Texture.

![](/ffaq/40/18/)

Another one east of Chaosdramon has, Rock Model Fragments.

![](/ffaq/40/19/)

The 3rd cart is to the south of him, at the end of the rubble, it contains Brown Texture.

![](/ffaq/40/20/)

Make your way east towards the objective but keep going ignoring the objective to find a chest in the corner containing, SP Capsules.

![](/ffaq/40/21/)

Now make your way to the objective. You can stay here and grind till you get all the digimon in the area or just to level up your mons it's up to you. Don't worry you can get these same mons later too.

Anyway take the elevator up.

Note: Carts respawn and will have different items when you exit and reenter an area (this might be why you end up with different items from me, they are essentially gather points for materials. From here on I will not mention carts as they are easy to see and all over the place.

#### 36F North Tower

Digimon Found  
---  
Keramon  
Kuramon  
Impmon  
Toyagumon  
Mushroomon  
  
From the elevator go south and then east, you will find a chest near the window containing, Character Reversal Skill Disk, it has a unique effect which reverses the usual attribute triangle. While equipped a Virus type is strong against Vaccines but weak against Data for example.

![](/ffaq/40/27/)

Continue south and then west, dealing with enemies as they come, when you arrive at the end Operator will explain Analyze mode, use it to reveal the damaged wall and use DigiAttack to destroy it.

Then continue north towards the objective and proceed west and a scene will play.

After that our path is blocked so go down the north hallway and you will see a Biyomon in the distance make your way to.

Break the wall leading to the office like rooms, then in the north west corner is a chest, it contains SP Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/28/)

From this chest go south east to find another one in a corner, it contains a DEF Enhancement.

![](/ffaq/40/29/)

Continue east until you have to destroy rubble to reach Biyomon.

After talking to Biyomon ask if it can take your across to the other side, and then make your way back to the destroyed hallway.

It goes......about as well you'd think.

#### 24F South Wing

Digimon Found  
---  
Keramon| Gazimon  
Kuramon| Gotsumon  
Kurisarimon|   
Tentomon|   
Motimon|   
  
Make your way west until you reach the end of the hall with a chest, it contains 2 HP Capsules.

![](/ffaq/40/30/)

Go south and then west just before you go down a slope Operator deigns to finally explain Digivolution.

You can now evolve or de-evolve any digimon that meet the correct conditions.

For example, Koromon can become Agumon if Agent Rank is 1 (Default) and Max HP is 530 or greater.  
Likewise it can become Botamon again if you have encountered Botamon at least once or it's personality matches the base personality of a Botamon.

Go ahead and test this out. You will notice the mons you evolve or de-evolve that you used in battle which have a higher bond have blue bracket stats, these are cumulative stats which carry over from the previous evolution or de-evolution, the amount of stats depends on the bond %. However you cannot carry over cumulative stat more than 100 times on a single mon.

Continue down the slope and then turn north, there will be a Kurisarimon here, it's a Champion digimon stronger than the others but with a decent team you should be able to handle it no problem.

Behind Kurisarimon is a chest containing 2 Revival Capsules.

![](/ffaq/40/31/)

Then go west and south to find another chest in a small room, it contains 2 HP Capsule I's.

![](/ffaq/40/32/)

Then analyze the collapsed wall and use Digiattack to proceed.

Go up the slope and go through the door to the stairs, DO NOT GO UP YET, go down stairs to a lower floor to find a lone chest here, it contains a HP Augment Chip I. This is an item which increases Cumulative Max HP.

![](/ffaq/40/33/)

Now go up stairs to the next area.

Note: If you go back and forth from these stairs and the next area you can grind Kurisarimon's Conversion rate and get a champion level earlier.

#### 36F South Tower

Digimon Found  
---  
Keramon| Toyagumon  
Kuramon| Impmon  
Kurisarimon|   
Mushroomon|   
Tentomon|   
  
Move west, the hall way has a crate that can be analyzed and destroyed (Analyze+DigiAttack) for a Speed Augment Chip I.

Make your west and then up the slope, until you reach a short scene.

Then turn west again to find a chest at the edge of the destroyed walkway, it contains Topaz, a jewel we can sell later.

![](/ffaq/40/34/)

Continue east in the northern hallway until you encounter a Kuramon and another Kurisarimon, behind the Kurisarimon is a chest, it contains the Hydrowater Skill Disk.

![](/ffaq/40/35/)

Then go down the hall further east and then south again to find another chest, this one has an attack augment chip I.

![](/ffaq/40/36/)

Continue to the stair case and go up to the next area.

#### Rooftop

Make your way to the rooftop and Operator will warn you to prepare yourself. Take their advice and get ready for a real boss fight.

##### Kuwagamon

Of course the first big boss we fight is Kuwagamon!

You will now be introduced to Cross Arts and how to gain CP.

On to brass tacks, Kuwagamon is an isectoid Champion level Virus digimon. Meaning you want fire wielding Vaccine types for this battle.
