Go back out and go down the elevator.

Then go south and melt the ice.

Now just make your way to the seed.

You will be brought back to the Terminal.

#### Redflame Area 2

Go east and cool the flame to the north for a chest, it contains Ignition Flare III.

![](/ffaq/40/230/)

Continue east and cool the fire to the south for another chest, this one has a DEF Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/231/)

Then go north to cool one more flame to find one more chest, it contains Medical Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/232/)

Go to the Yellowflame area and make your way to the next area, by cooling the flames at the end.

#### Whiteflame Area

MudFrigimons will roll down the hill, you can DigiAttack them, touching them causes battle.

Once you reach the first platform there is a chest, it contains an SP Augment chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/233/)

When you make it to the top you will have a battle against Megidramon, Meramon and Mudfrigimon.

After the battle there are 2 chests in this wide open space, they contain Feather Attachment II and Full Revival Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/234/)

Then go to the next area, the Throne Room, make your way to the top of the throne for a scene.

You will then have a boss fight against Apollomon.

##### Apollomon

He is a Vaccine type, as a Fire element digimon he is VERY weak to Ice. Data type mega levels with Ice skills will be very effective.

He is a very offensive fighter, as he takes damage he will speed up his CP growth rate. He uses physical attacks so reflecting them with refelct skills can be useful.

After he goes below 50% he will buff ALL his stats immensely and cast magic reflect on himself.

Debuffing him or using skills which steal or reverse his buffs can be handy here.

When he near defeat he will start charging up, aim for the sphere on his back, if you Cross Arts: Strike ready just use it to instantly break him.

This will cause the sphere momentum to increase though, so finish him or he will quickly regain CP.

After the battle follow Apollomon out of Heat Cosmic, you'll need to move through Yellowflame part way until a scene plays.

Go south east and clear the flames, go down the ladder, follow the path clear some more flames and then go to the elevator.

After the scene we will be back at the Terminal.

Return to Chill Cosmic and make your way to Crescent Moon.

#### Crescent Moon Area 2

Go east to find an elevator blocked by flames, cool them go up the elevator.

Move south and then west to find a chest containing, Medical Spray DX.

![](/ffaq/40/236/)

Go east, clear a flame and then continue to the elevator.

Once up clear the flame and go down the ice slide.

Then go towards the exit to the north east, right before the exit is a chest containing Defense Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/237/)

#### Halfmoon Area

This is the same as the Whiteflame area, there will be Penmon rolling down on you as you climb up.

Reach the first landing and go north to find a chest containing Hydro Water III.

![](/ffaq/40/238/)

Continue up the slope to the right of the 2nd group of penmons is another chest, it contains a Sleep Wall I.

![](/ffaq/40/239/)

Continue up to find another chest with a Curse Barrier Skill Disk.

![](/ffaq/40/240/)

Make your way past the last group of penmons and you'll fight Vikemon.

Now finally make your way to the throne room and go up to the throne for some scenes and a boss fight. Have Virus digimon with fire skills have them equip Sleep Wall and Attachments which prevent sleep.

##### Dianamon

She is the opposite of Apollomon. She is weak to Fire and is a Data type, so she is weak to virus types.

She can put your digimon to sleep, so having sleep wall or similar equipment will help.

Once all your digimon are asleep she will start charging immediately, luckily even if this does happen

She will continue this loop throughout the battle, only occasionally attacking outside this method.

It's a pretty easy fight overall if you come prepared.

After the battle is over we will be back at the Terminal with a new objective.

Before that though, go back to Dianamon's Throne Room.

You will find the FINAL Outer Dungeon behind her throne. This is the last one there is to find in the game and if you have beaten or cleared the others this is also the final trial.

##### Protect The Giant Digimeat 4

Same deal as all prior versions.

This time will be pretty challenging as more of them will spawn at once, furthermore you have flying types and fast types.

Frigimons will roll very fast so they are impossible to catch when they pass you, Wingdramons will have some time before they go up into the air buying you time to hit them.

Frigimon and Wingdramon will ignore you while Panjyamon and IceDevimon will chase you to force you into battle.

If you stay vigilant in front of the enemy line this should be very doable.

Note: If you are having trouble defeating enemies in single hits and some are taking too many hits using up time, then level up the digimon you are using to ensure they can finish the enemy in one hit. Level 40 to 60 generally is a good place to beat this challenge without issue.

The message crystal is earned if you beat this trial without being forced into a battle.

The reward digimon is Eaglemon.

With that we have completed all Outer dungeons, if you have beaten all the Outer dungeons you will get a final message from the Game Master and an achievement (even if you don't do get all the message crystals those are a bonus).

When ready to proceed leave and go back to MasterBlimpmon and go to the Guardian Palace.

When you arrive you will be sent a message by Volcamon. So we're gonna take a quick detour.

##### The Greatest OMNI Carnival Continued

Go back to the arena in Factorial Area and fight the Gatekeepers of Fire and Ice.

This will be a dual battle against Wizardmon and Sorcermon.

And we're done for now, return to the Guardian Palace.

Make your way inside to face Junomon.

Some lengthy scenes will play.

#### Palace Otherside: East Passage

Digimon Found  
---  
Wisemon  
Unimon  
Gatomon  
  
JAIL BREAK!

After the scenes go south and speak to Dianamon.

Then go north and ClavisAngemon will appear causing a battle.

The angels here are boosted abnormally, our own stats are lowered but the angels have more HP are buffered and can counter physical.

Their evasion in particular is high, after a point your hits won't land. Dianamon will put him to sleep and the fight will end as you escape.

Go west for a scene, then go up the stairs. On the 2nd floor go around the stairs for a chest, it contains Needle Plant II.

![](/ffaq/40/241/)

Then examine the device nearby. Continue south and you'll find a chest in the next open space, it contains Full Revival Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/242/)

Follow the objective south and then upstairs to the next area.

When you arrive on the bridge turn around to find a chest next to the entrance, it contains Vigor Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/255/)

Cross the bridge to find a chest on the other side, it contains an SPI Augment Chip III. Continue to the next area.

![](/ffaq/40/243/)

#### West Passage

After the scenes go down the stairs and go north and then west and speak to Ogremon through the wall.

The walls are blocking the way forward to remove the walls go to the point shown below and look up and DigiAttack the eye like structure.

![](/ffaq/40/244/)

This will also reveal a chest behind the wall, it contains an HP Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/245/)

Then go to where Ogremon and co are, there is a chest next to them here too, it contains a Medical Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/246/)

Activate the Beta Switch, leave and the angel will appear, make your way towards the exit but the path is blocked so look up and DigiAttack the eye thing again.

Then go south all the way to reach the Alpha Device, flip the switch.

You will then be trapped in the room, look up and DigiAttack the eye.

ClavisAngemon will pop up, run from him as fighting him is a pain and not worth it even if you somehow beat him (it is possible) and he will just respawn soon after.

Make your way through the hall as your path is changed until you reach a dead end, attack the eye thing above again.

Then you will find a chest to the west of this large room, it contains Saint Knuckle III Skill Disk.

![](/ffaq/40/247/)

Make your north to the next hall way, this is the same but this time as you move ClavisAngemon will reappear in several places.

You will eventually reach a fork of sorts with ClavisAngemon in the center, go left to find the eye and DigiAttack it. Then go back to the fork and go to the right to find a chest containing Shadow Fang III.

![](/ffaq/40/248/)

Return to the middle area and a scene will play. The angel will appear again, ignore him and take the east exit out of this area.

Cross the bridge and we will return to the East Passage.

#### East Passage 2

Pull the lever, to open the door and now return to the Gamma Device and activate it.

Then go downstairs, there will be a scene then turn around and run downstairs.

This will be your chance to defeat ClavisAngemon at last, it won't hard now that his buffs and stats are gone.

Time will be frozen after the fight before pulling the switch go behind him and check his back pocket for ClavisAngemon's Charm.

Pull the switch.

Then go back upstairs, check the cell where Kuzuhamon was kept to find a chest containing X Aura.

![](/ffaq/40/251/)

Next go into the cell where Pharaohmon was and you'll find a chest containing ATK Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/250/)

Then go south to the back end cells, when you enter go east to find a chest, this chest has a SPD Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/252/)

Then go check the cell on the west side above Minervamon's cell for another chest, this one has an HP Attachment III.

![](/ffaq/40/253/)

Then go speak to Minervamon.

After the scenes you will be in the center area, and can go towards Junomon's throne room again, but for now we'll go back to the East Passage.

Go to the south end of the upper floor and near the south most stairs you will find a chest SP Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/254/)

The West Passage now has enemies.

Digimon Found  
---  
Wisemon  
Unimon  
Gatomon  
MagnaAngemon  
Angewoman  
  
When ready to proceed go to Junomon's Chamber. Where you will face a boss.

For this boss prepare digimon that can undo debuffs or equipment which prevents them. As well as Vaccine digimon with Light attacks.

##### Junomon HM (Hysteric Mode)

A Mega level Virus type. You will need a pretty beefed up party to handle her.

Vaccine types with Light are VERY effective against her.

During the battle the temple will debuff you initially your accuracy and then all other stats as the fight progresses.

Items will also be sealed off. She will gain CP and keep debuffing you but won't use any charged attacks or at least you can defeat her long before it comes to that.

Watch the scenes and leave the theater.

## Walkthrough

### Chapter 19: Agent Alpha

#### Previous Area clean up

Before we proceed return to the digital world.

First go to the Guardian Temple (Junomon area), the card shop is back and has new cards.

##### The Greatest OMNI Carnival continued

We have a new match up now. Select Hey Kid! You here for a fight?.

You'll be fighting Titamon in the same form as his boss fight, albi much easier to beat, also when he takes enough damage  
he reverts to in-training. **  
** **Once you speak to Junomon the game will proceed into the finale!  
** **But it is not the point of no return....yet!**

#### Clash of Titans

When ready to proceed return to Rebellion village and speak to Junomon

Once you do some lengthy scenes will play.

When you regain control approach the shadow, follow it and continue the interactive scenes to their conclusion.

You will then begin the final battle against the Giant Slayer.

You will only control Jupitermon WM with only Attack available. This a fully scripted battle.

#### Akashic Records Periphery

After the end of this battle and some long scenes. You will regain control.

Make your way to through the area follow the tracks until a scene plays.

Then approach Biyomon nearby for more scenes.

Here we go again.

#### Akashic Hades

Once you end up here open the doors.

There will be a scene when you open the door, there is a chest right in front of you it contains a Damage Down, this equipment nullifies any damage that is 10% or less than your Max HP.

![](/ffaq/40/256/)

Go up the slope towards Plutomon you'll find another chest behind the wall, it contains an HP Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/257/)

When you reach near the top there is a door leading to the theater, enter cuz we have a side quest.

##### A Gift from the Hosts

Enter the VIP room.

After the scene the hosts will grant you the ability to visit any time and area you have visited.

Thus you can visit areas 8 years in the past as well as those in the present.

This sub quest is finished..........except then you get a bajillion MORE!

#### Pre-Finale Side Questing

##### Encounters Beyond Space Time

The first side quest is from Mirei so speak to her in the Theater. Simply speaking to her completes the quest.

However this unlocks 4 Outer Dungeons called Ultimate Battles.

These can be considered super bosses or just post game bosses, their levels are in the 70s so and their stats ARE VERY HIGH.  
Unless your digimon are quite strong and suped up maybe hold off until after the final boss.

They can be refought.

##### Ultimate Battle Apocalymon

He is a fought like a standard enemy but his Darkness Zone erases 90% of the HP of all party members.

He will also use AOE confusion skills. Equipping anti confuse equipment is helpful.

Maintain HP by healing with X Aura and you should be fine. (Assuming your digimon are around level 50 to 60 minimum).

Aside from this he uses various dark skills. He has no charging or CP.

##### Ultimate Battle Agumon (Bond of Bravery)

A very easy battle. He is very weak to Ice skill.

He does have a few hard hitting moves but if your level is adequate and you have a good team of megas you'll be just fine!

##### Ultimate Battle Gabumon (Bond of Friendship)

Another easy battle. He is weak to Wind skills. He doesn't even hit as hard as Agumon.

##### Ultimate Battle Imperialdramon DM

A little bit tougher than the prior two but not as tough as Apocalymon.

Imperialdramon has high attack powers and deals a lot of damage, his main moves are light oriented. He has several weaknesses, such as Water and Wind but they aren't severe weaknesses.

Enter the VIP room and King Drasil will speak to you about assembling the Royal Knights.  
This will trigger the Royal Knights Assemble! quest.

When you leave the room you'll be in central tower in the present this will also cause a large number of new quests to appear.  
All of these related to one of the missing Royal knights.

You can now also challenge Agumon and Gabumon to card battles.

##### Knowing One's Strength

Just go down the stairs from where Agumon and Gabumon are and speak to Craniamon.

Then make your way to Shinjuku (Present Day), specifically the Coffee shop outside Kabukicho.

Note: Yet another side quest will pop up when you arrive in Shinjuku. I'll cover that one later for now go to the Coffee Shop.

Speak to Craniamon diguised as a human speaking with the Father of Shinjuku (AncientWisemon).

Go back out and go to the Subroad in the underground mall/station you, go to the west hallway next to the Cietra Cafe Restaurant.  
You'll find a man here, speak to him.

Then agree to go to the Dark Field.

Go up to the castle, (you'll uh notice Hiroko here for some reason, ignore this one for now this is another long side quest we'll do later).

Speak to Plutomon. This will complete this side quest but a new one will rear it's head.

##### Showdown with the Immortal Steed

Go back outside the castle and speak to Craniamon again.

You will then fight Enbarrmon. It isn't a hard fight.

After the battle the mission is complete.

Another side quest will immediately pop up.

##### Secrets are for Sharing

Speak to the Bakemon behind you.

Go inside the castle and speak to Imperialdramon.

Then just report back to Bakemon, this will complete the quest. It will also reward you with the Fragment of Memory of Plutomon.

On to the next Royal Knight. Go to Factorial Town.

##### One Shield Maker's Struggle

Speak to Gallantmon.

After the scenes we will be fighting Chaosdramon. He is pretty easy, just lower his HP to half and the fight ends.

Then after a scene, he will return. Just beat him and the mission is done.

Now go to the Paradise Colosseum for the next quest.

Before we do that the arena has a few new fights:

##### The Greatest OMNI Carnival continued again

You have 3 new fights ready, start with The Guardian's Guardians.

You will fight MagnaAngemon, SlashAngemon and ClavisAngemon.

Next is Golchidrain, defeat Chirinmon and Goldramon.

Then we have Lucemon 2/3, defeat Seraphimon and Ophanimon.

Not done yet, another team will pop up, Terrier Twins and Their Teacher. Defeat Terriermon, Lopmon and Cherubimon (Good).

With that we're done, now just speak to the Royal Knight in the arena.

##### A Stubborn Fighter? + Yaro-Kei Ramen

Speak to Gankoomon in the arena.

Doing so will unlock another Side quest which must be completed first.

Then go to Akihabara to the alley, (the card shop in the alley has new cards).

Go north of the Attachment shop and speak to Jesmon.

This will complete this side quest, then go back to Gankoomon.

Gankoomon will then join the Arena as Might Mad Man.

Defeat him and then this quest will complete but a new one will pop up.

##### Choosting a Human Representative

Go to Akihabara Back Alley and speak to Gankoomon.

After the scenes defeat Kuzuhamon and the mission will end.

Next go to the Gear Forest, Santuary and speak to Veemon

##### Veemon's Growth

Speak to Veemon and then agree to take him someplace.

The quest will end after some scenes. Then 2 more will pop up.

Then go to Factorial Area again and speak to Magnamon.

##### Bring your own Materials

Speak to Magnamon. Watch the scenes and then you'll fight Breakdramon.

The quest will end once he is beaten.

A new quest will now pop up, we'll do this one later.

Now go to Abyss Sanctuary and speak to UlforceVeedramon.

##### The Worthy

Speak to UlforceVeedramon and then after a scene you will fight Neptunemon.

This is a quick and easy fight.

You will be brought to the Factorial area afterwards, speak to UlforceVeedramon again and the side quest is finished.

Our next stop is the Shaded Woods in Gear Forest, speak to Lalamon.

 **Note: By now you have more than enough points to reach Agent Rank 8 as such several new evolutions should be available for MEGA+ digimon.**

##### A Facilitator's Woes

Speak to Lalamon and then make your way to the objective, you'll find a wandering Leopardmon LM running around like a normal enemy.

Enter a battle, defeat him and this quest will end.

Yet two more quests will rear their heads.

Next go to Akihabara. Speak to the man near the disk shop.

##### The Pinnacle of the Card World

The man will challenge you to a card fight, defeat him.

Then go to the shopping district and speak to the young man in a white hoodie.

(ITS THAT KID!) Note: This is the only opponent who has the Aegiomon card.

At the end of the quest you'll get a blank card, then go to any card shop and you can buy an Ukkomon card for exchanging the Blank Card.

The card shop in Central Town will now have new cards, specifically Aegiochusmon cards.

The card shop in Guardian Palace now has 2 new cards as well namely Chronomon HM and DM.

Speak to Bacchusmon in Gear Forest (Present).

##### Find a Lead Vocalist!

Speak to Bacchusmon, you'll notice Leopardmon is here too.

Then go to the sanctuary and speak to Leopardmon again, then you'll fight GranKuwagamon.

This will complete the quest, a new one will pop up which we'll do a bit later.

Now go to the Misono residence, Kosuke's old office.

##### Duels must be Fair

Speak to Hiroko and choose to investigate the phase electron lifeforms.

You will then fight Dynasmon and then fight a Megadramon.

This will complete the quest, and just as you do another one will pop.

##### Blink and you'll Miss It

Go to Akihabara and speak to Shiroki. After the scene speak to Simmons 3 times until a scene plays.

Then you'll fight Kentaurosmon.

This quest will be finished but another one will pop up.

Go up the central tower (you'll see all the knights you have gathered so far), you can challenge Craniamon, Enbarrmon and Gallantmon to card battles.

##### The Beautiful Knight Corps

Go to Central Tower and speak to Dynasmon.

Then go to Akihabara Back Alley and speak to LordKnightmon.

You will then fight 3 sets of 3 Knightmon, and then defeat Lobomon.

The quest will end and a new one will pop.

Go back to Center Tower again.

##### A Display of Beautiful Swordplay

Speak to LordKnightmon. After the scene speak to him again.

Then go to Paradise Colosseum and speak to LordKnightmon again.

This will unlock the Solo Beauty match in the arena.

Defeat LordKnightmon and this will end the quest.

Return to the tower, and on the west side you will see a hidden digimon talking with a speech bubble, speak to them.

##### The Call of the Unseen

Speak to a very tiny Examon.

After the scenes you will fight HerculesKabuterimon and Kuwagamon.

This quest will end after this.

With that all Royal Knights are gathered at the tower (don't worry about Alphamon).

Also we now have enough Agent Points to reach Rank 9.

At rank 9 you can evolve your own version of Aegiomon as well all his forms including Jupitermon, however they each have a requirement of maxing out different personality bond skills, so you likely will be limited on what you can do until reaching Rank 10.

In any case return to the Theater. Report back to King Drasil in the VIP room.

This will complete Royal Knights Assemble! You will receive the Stats Wall Attachment for this (and another Immortal egg).  
The former prevents debuffs.

A new quest will also pop up, so go meet the knights on top of Central Tower.

##### Alpha and Omega

Speak to Agumon.

You will then fight Alphamon. This will complete this quest.

A new quest will now pop up.

Next we'll go to the Tavern in Central Town.

##### Final Battle Challenger

Speak to Rapidmon and Pharaohmon.

This will add Golden Intruders to the Arena. Go there and defeat them.

That will end this quest.

##### Memories of Bonds

Go to Akihabara and north of the Skill Disk shop is a woman, in a white shirt. Speak to her for the next quest.

Then go to the allye and you'll find Shiroki and Beelstarmon.

Watch the scenes and the quest will end

Next go to the Theater and go to Gear Forest 8 years ago and speak to Punimon

##### A Stubborn Fog

Note: This might be a quest you (or I) missed during the 1st half of the game.

Speak to Punimon. Then speak to Cherrymon.

After he refuses DigiAttack him, then fight him until it is broken up by Sirenmon.

Then defeat MegaKabuterimon.

Next go and speak to Hiroko in Kosuke's office again.

##### Scariest Face in the World

Speak to Hiroko and then after the creepy scenes, make your way to the objective in the alley and enter the Outer Dungeon.

Then defeat Wendigomon.

This quest is complete, a new quest will pop up though.

Go back to the office and speak to Hiroko again.

##### Fulfilling a Great Ambition

Speak to Hiroko for a scene.

You will then have a fight with Millenniummon. After more scenes you will fight him again.

A new quest will pop up after this one finishes up.

##### Observing the Underworld

Speak to Hiroko again. Now go back in time to 8 years ago in Shinjuku park.

Then speak to the guy in the middle of the park.

Go to the sub road and find Hiroko and Shiroki.

You'll be in the alley after that go straight north to find Simmons.

Return to the present, go to Overbridge Intersection (follow the objective marker) and speak to Kinshiro in a suit.

This will complete the quest.

Go to Kosuke's old office again and speak to Hiroko about the letter.

##### An Odd Letter

Speak to Hiroko and then after the scene go to the Wall of Hope.

You will then have a battle and can choose which of the Royal Knights to aid you.

Defeat Machinedramon (Level 70 can be tough if your level is too low but not likely at this point).

Another new quest should pop up now.

Return once more to the top of Central Tower to meet the knights.

##### Royal Knights' Test Match

Speak to Agumon, this will complete the quest. 

That said now you can speak to and fight any and all of the Royal Knights. 

Do not take them lightly these are the strongest bosses yet all around level 85.

Defeating them all will receive an acknowledgement (and alter some of the final battle scenes).

You can refight them as much as you want and it can serve as some good grinding for weak digimon, or testing out builds.

With this we are done with any Royal Knight related quests.

You can also buy are Royal Knight cards from the card shop in Akihabara.

At this point it should be possible to fully complete your Card Collection using the cards in shops in each region.

Next we'll complete any Olympus XII quests.

##### To My Dearest

Speak to Neptunemon in this throne room.

Go to Venusmon in one of the houses in Central Town.

You will then return to Neptunemon and the quest will end.

##### Longing for Akihabara

Go to Factorial Town 8 years in the past and speak to Vulcanusmon.

Then go to Shinjuku 8 years ago to Akihabara, find Simmons by going north from the west wall.

Then speak to the store keeper. This quest will end.

##### Fertilizer for the Soul

Now go to the Sanctuary in Gear Forest in the present.

Speak to Sirenmon's sprout that will become Ceresmon. Then go to the village and speak to ShogunGekomon.

Go to the Shaded Woods and collect 3 Spirit Seeds marked on the map and report back.

This will complete the quest, you will also get the Fruit of Karpos Hule.

##### What is Love?

Go to Central Town Downtown and speak to Minervamon.

After some scenes the quest will end. Once you leave the room, a new quest will pop up.

##### Premonitions of Love

Speak to Minvermon in Rebellion Village.

then talk to Tanemon and now be Card battles it.

This serves as a hint for what Minervamon finds........Worthy.

It's Beelzemon of course!

You will now need to bring your own Beelzemon to Minervamon, this may take a bit depending on how far along you are in terms evolving digimon or building stats. 

A tip for reaching evolutions easily: Build up cumualative stats of a single mon by loading several converted digimon (let them level up a bit) to raise the cumulative stats over 2000 this will allow it to move through evolutions easily. 

Bring a Beelzemon to Minveramon and then she will run off.

Then go find her nearby.

##### Triumph is Mine

Leave the area and come back (go to Merukimon's room). Then speak to Minervamon again.

Then you will fight Minveramon.

After defeating her the quest will end. Return to Central Town and a new quest will pop

##### Minervamon's Decision

Speak to Minervamon in her house again.

Then you'll be brought to a Beelzemon in Factorial Area. Bring him an Iron Texture which you probably already have.

You will then have a battle with Beelzemon Blast Mode.

Defeating him will complete the quest.

Now for the next quest go to the theater and enter the VIP room and speak to Kunlun.

This will begin the Helpers from Shambala quest line.

##### The Forgetful Helpers

Go to the Paradise Colosseum and speak to Kotemon.

Then fight them in the arena as Master and Pupil Trio. Defeat Kotemon, Musyamon and Zanbamon.

Speak to them again and this quest will end and the next will pop.

##### Susanomon The Hero

Speak to Susanomon. 

Go to the Arena and fight him as Hero of Shambala. Defeat Susanomon.

Speak to him again afterwards. This quest will be completed.

Go back to Kunlun to complete the Helpers from Shambala quest line.

What we have next are 3 collection type side quests which involve locating certain NPCs or enemies.

We'll start with the Etemon one.

##### All Eyes on Me!

This quest spawns when you first arrive in Shinjuku 8 years ago. 

Etemon has lost pieces of his soul and their scattered throughout the area. Find Etemon in various parts of the city. Below are the locations with images. When you find them DigiAttack them to collect.

  1.  **Back Alley (the one connected to the shopping district), south parking lot.**  
![](/ffaq/40/258/)
  2.  **In the shopping district near the Clothes shop.**  
![](/ffaq/40/259/)
  3.  **Found just a bit south of the Skill Disk Shop in Akihabara  
![](/ffaq/40/260/)**
  4.  **Found lying down at the Taxi Parking in the Back Alley in Akihabara  
![](/ffaq/40/261/)  
**
  5.  **In between the trees in the middle of Vision Square  
![](/ffaq/40/262/)  
**
  6.  **Hanging from the top of the west tunnel at the Overbridge Intersection  
![](/ffaq/40/263/)  
**
  7.  **Hanging from a tree near the south end of Koshu Kaido Intersection  
![](/ffaq/40/264/)**
  8.  **Right outside the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building  
![](/ffaq/40/265/)  
**
  9.  **On the east side of Shinjuku Park, hanging from a tree.  
![](/ffaq/40/266/)**
  10.  **Inside the Coffee Shop found outside Kabukicho at Koshu-Kaido Intersection  
![](/ffaq/40/267/)  
**
  11.  **Found at the south end of the underground town, subroad.  
![](/ffaq/40/268/)  
**
  12.  **At the Marunonaka line, go down to the platform and find him lying down at the north end  
![](/ffaq/40/269/)**
  13.  **Right in the middle of the Underground Promenade.  
![](/ffaq/40/270/)**
  14.  **Immediately visible when you enter Shinjuku Station East Gate.  
![](/ffaq/40/271/)**
  15.  **Shinjuku Underground Waterway, South Block, found in the 2nd open space after entering via Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building Entrance  
![](/ffaq/40/272/)  
**
  16.  **Shinjuku Underground Waterway, North Block. In the water control area where we chased Coo, he is lying face up in the water.  
![](/ffaq/40/273/)  
**
  17.  **Shinjuku Underground Resevoir, in front of the closed door to the north west.  
![](/ffaq/40/274/)**



Return to the Theater and the mission will auto complete, you will get Etemon;s bling an equip item which reduces damage from virus types by 30%.

Now go to Shinjuku in the Present Day.

##### Silent Envy

That moron Kuga unleashed a bunch of Kuramon on the city. Just like with the Etemon they are scattered 1 per area.  
They are marked on your maps as red dots, so you can find them easily and one shot them with digiattacks. Here is a check list of areas:

  1. North east dead end in Back Alley connected to the Shopping District
  2. Outside Misono Residence in the shopping district
  3. Between the Taxi and Skill Disk shop in Akihabara
  4. In the Alley at Akihabara, check the garbage dump north of Weekly Mart.
  5. Vision Square on the west end of the trees.
  6. In the eastern tunnel at the Overbridge Intersection.
  7. Koshu-Kaido Intersection north of Weekly Mart.
  8. Kabukicho, outside the arcade, in the wide open area where Locomon is.
  9. At the Wall of Hope.
  10. In the eastern hallway of the Underground Town Subroad.
  11. West side of the upper area of the Marunonaka Line.
  12. West end of Underground Promenade
  13. Shinjuku Station East Gate, near the train gate.



Return to the Theater and the quest will auto complete.

Now for the last one.

##### Dreams of Reality?

There are these weird Hiroko clones throughout the digital world. We need to find them. When we do find them and talk to them they will evaporate leaving behind a message of their experiences. They are found in both past and present.

Below are the locations

  1.  **Central Town 8 Years ago: Central Tower Steel Point, North end looking over railing.  
![](/ffaq/40/275/)**
  2.  **Abyss Area 8 Years ago: Abyss Depths, south west end where we meet Sumarimon  
![](/ffaq/40/276/)  
**
  3.  **Gear Forest 8 years ago: Sanctuary, right in front of the Current/Great Tree, lying on the ground.  
![](/ffaq/40/277/)**
  4.  **Factorial Area 8 years ago: In the maze like area where you helped Kokuwamon rescue trapped digimon.  
She can be found where you helped Tankmon and Mechnorimon.  
![](/ffaq/40/278/)  
**
  5.  **Guardian Palace 8 years ago: Standing posing at the beginning of the hallway leading to Junomon's throne.  
![](/ffaq/40/279/)  
**
  6.  **Central Town Present: At the very bottom of the sewers in the north west corner.  
![](/ffaq/40/280/)  
**
  7.  **Cosmic Area: Chill Cosmic Throne room, Behind the throne just sitting on the ice.  
![](/ffaq/40/281/)  
**
  8.  **Abyss Sanctuary: In the north east end of the area, where the jail cells are, where Sharkmon and later JumboGamemon were kept.  
![](/ffaq/40/282/)  
**
  9.  **Gear Forest Present: Floating Island Plains, she is at the Locomon Station on the platform with Lotusmon  
![](/ffaq/40/283/)  
**
  10.  **Factorial Area Present: Factorial Core (Vulcanusmon's old room), she is in the middle of the room looking at the exhibit.  
![](/ffaq/40/284/)  
**
  11.  **Guardian Palace Present: Inside Junomon's Throne Room to her left  
![](/ffaq/40/285/)  
**
  12.  **Dark Field: In the Dark Garden, just outside the door to Plutomon's Castle  
![](/ffaq/40/286/)  
**
  13.  **Rebellion Village, Standing on the upper level of the north west side of the village  
![](/ffaq/40/287/)  
**



Then return to the theater and the quest will auto complete.

Thats EVERYTHING!

You'll notice we still have Agent Points to earn, thats because we'll have to wait till post game to reach rank 10.

For now do anything else you want to prepare for the final battle, train your mons, stock up on items, complete the card collection or as much of the Field Guide as you can.

Remember the powerful Accessory items you can create from Guardromon (Gold) now that you have the Fragment Quest Items.  
(You're limited by the number of Immortal eggs you collected)

When ready return to the Real world by selecting the option when leaving the Theater.

## Walkthrough

### Chapter 20: Finale

#### Akashic Hades

Once your digimon are ready return here at last. I recommend ensuring your party has effectively high stats 5000+ or around there.

Suggested level is 75+

As the boss's mechanics are unique even among bosses in this game.

In any case speak to Plutomon to proceed.

Then speak to Gekomon, you will then fight Barbamon, Skullgreymon and Infermon.

After the scenes go up to the steps to collect a chest, it contains Energy Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/288/)

Then speak to Gekomon to proceed.

#### Akashic Chaos

When you arrive here there is a chest to the left it contains an SPI Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/289/)

Go up the stairs and you'll fight Machinedramon and Kimeramon.

After more scenes you will fight Creepymon and 2 Millenniummons.

Once the battle and some more scenes pass speak to Pegasusmon to continue.

#### Akashic Forest

Once you arrive and watch a quick scene go straight to find a chest below you, it contains a Revival Capsule DX.

![](/ffaq/40/290/)

Go up the branch to the right and follow the objective, you will reach a cliff and Rosemon and Palmon will show up to help.

Use Palmon to swing across to the left, go up the branch until you reach another landing, swing to the right this time and you will find another chest here, it contains a Destruction Attachment.

![](/ffaq/40/291/)

Go back down to the landing where Rosemon showed up and swing to the right this time to reach the Red fruit. Attempt a DigiAttack for a scene and then DigiAttack again.

Now go back down to the fruit.

From Bacchusmon go back to the tree but go left this time and swing across using Palmon.

Knock down the Blue fruit, then go to the fruit.

Some Cerberusmon will appear around the fruit, defeat them, pick up the fruit and bring it to Inori.

Go back up the right side of the tree again and when you reach the first landing swing to the left, go up all the way to the next landing and swing left again and then one more swing to reach the fruit.

Knock it down and head back down again.

When you reach the fruit you will enter a battle against Lilithmon and 2 Cerberusmon.

Then go through the opened path and on the way edge you'll find a chest just before Whamon, it contains SP augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/292/)

Go down the branch to Whamon to move to the next area.

#### Akashic Abyss

Make your way forward for a scene.

Then follow the path to the Kamemon. Then break the coral nearby. 

Proceed through and to the right is another coral to destroy this one is hiding a chest containing Full Revival Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/293/)

Continue onwards and you'll fight Infermon and 2 Kurisarimon.

After the fight go around the Seadramons to the left to find a chest, it contains an ATK Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/294/)

Then go up the stairs to reach Submarimon. Use him to go through the water current hall, you'll then fight 2 Diaboromon.

Go through the next water current and watch the scenes.

Then open the chest next to the pillar it has a Destruction Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/295/)

Destroy the wall and defeat an Infermon, then you'll get some back up.

After that go through the hall and to the left destroy some coral to reveal a chest containing DEF Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/296/)

Continue on the path to reach Neptunemon.

You will then fight Leviamon.

Once he is defeated go through the gate to the next area.

#### Akashic Oblivion

In this area there are 2 chests to the left and right, they contain Medical Spray DX and INT Augment Chip III.

![](/ffaq/40/297/)

You need to cross the invisible bridge and get the batteries from the Tsumemon, to see the bridge use Analyze.

You will fall if you go over the edge, this also means you can jump down to areas below.

Once you get the battery you will automatically go back.

You will then fight Lucemon Satan Mode.

Go through the gate after that.

#### Akashic Ragnorak

Go up to Apollomon, Dianamon and Minervamon. You will need to defend the 3 while they charge up.

This is basically the same as the Protect The DigiMeat Outer Dungeon trials, use Digiattack to defeat enemies.

After overcoming the waves you will battle Beelzemon Blast Mode.

Then go through the gate.

#### Akashic Maze

You will be in a warped Central Town, go towards Downtown and enter the building.

You will then be inside the Misono Residence, go through the door here and you will be brought to Factorial Town.

Go through another door to go to the Shopping District, then enter Kosuke's office. 

You will be back in Central Town, having gone in a circle.

Go to the Skill Disk shop on the roof is Belphemon Sleep Mode, DigiAttack it.

Make your way back to Factorial Town, but don't go through the door, instead stop and look to the right of the door to see Beelzemon Sleep Mode, attack it again.

Then go to the Misono Residence and attack the one in the living room.

In this shopping district he is in front of the fruit stands.

Return to Factorial Town version and you will him on the right this time.

Once all 5 of them are destroyed the door will take you to the Tavern.

You will then fight Belphemon in both his modes.

Once he is beaten we'll be out of this weird place.

##### Before the Final Battle (Point of no return)

This is it, all your allies have been gathered and we are at Chronomon's doorstep.

You can access the Theater for any final prep.

While in the theater a message from Volcamon will pop and you can go to the Arena and fight the Kings of Pharaohmon. Defeat PrinceMamemon, Pharaohmon and KingEtemon.

This will then allow you to fight the final opponent, Marsmon, God of DigiBattles.

Once he is beaten you will have completed the Arena Side quest.

When ready speak to Aegiomon for the final battle.

#### Chronomon

The battle will begin by his buffing ALL his stats to an absurd degree, even his resistances to all elements. Minervamon will join the battle.

You won't be able to do much damage at all during this initial part of the fight regardless of what attack you use.

Just hang in there until a scene plays.

After this his barrier will be broken, Neptunemon, Merukimon and Venusmon will join the battle.

You can go ham attacking him now, however his attacks now include moves which debuff your party and cause confusion so use items to undo them.

Once he takes enough damage he will forcefully skip your party's turns and attack early, this will do a lot of damage (this damage is percantage based).  
He will also recover all the damage you did the last few turns.  
Neptunemon and the rest will swap out with Marsmon, Bacchusmon and Vulcanusmon.

Again once you deal enough damage he will control time to skip your turns, reverse his damage and do a lot of damage.  
Marsmon's group will swap out with Plutomon and Junomon.

Once again after you have dealt enough damage he will control time, skip turns and deal damage, forcing Plutomon and Junomon to retreat.

A scene will then play.

He will then use Chrono Devolution.

This will devolve your digimon to in-training, Chronomon will also return to full health.

While your digimon are devolved, their stats remain unchanged, so if your stats are high you can still deal quite a bit of damage even in this state. All your equipped Skill Disks are still intact.

Dianamon and Apollomon will join the battle now.

After a turn Aegiomon will use his power to reverse the devolution reverting your mons to Rookie level.

On every turn one of his turns now he will evolve your mons up one level.

Either once all your mons return to their original state or his HP hits 0, a scene will play. The fight will continue with Minervamon joining the battle.

Once again if his HP hits 0 or if enough turns pass another scene will play.

Minvervamon will evolve into Mervamon, the fight will continue if he has HP left which he likely won't at this point.

When it's time to end it use a X-Arts powered by the Olympus XII.

Now sit back and enjoy the finale and credits, as this story comes to a close.

#### Post Game

After the credits you can save Clear Save Data.

Loading this save data will return you to before the final boss.

You will receive the Anomaly Points from completing the final boss, and the final side quest will pop up.

##### Olympos XII's Test Match

Go to Akihabara Back Alley (Present Day) and speak to Minervamon.

Just speakiing to her will complete the mission and grant you the remaining Anomaly points you are missing.

You can now reach Agent Rank 10. This will allow you to Evolve an Elecmon into Aegiomon, and then into any of his forms.

From those forms you can also evolve him into Jupitermon.  
(Aegiochusmon Dark can only become Chronomon DM though).

Jupitermon can then evolve further to Jupitermon WM.

Lastly, you can evolve Butenmon (an Ultimate level digimon) into Chronomon HM and Chronomon DM.

When ready enter the Outer Dungeon. 

Just like with the Royal knights you can speak to any member of the Olympus XII here and fight them.

However, unlike then you'll be fighting them all in a Gauntlet.

For this battle I recommend using the Cross Art: High Field. It's buffing power allows you to fight with close to no restraint as all your stats are buffed.

Starting with Apollomon, Dianamon and Minvervamon.

When one is defeated another takes it's place, Minervamon will become Mervamon when defeated.  
Once both Apollomon and Dianamon are beaten GraceNovamon will join the battle.

Marsmon will then fill the remaining spot.

The order after this is: Merukimon, Vulcanusmon, Junomon, Venusmon, Neptunemon, Bacchusmon, Ceresmon.

Jupitermon will appear at the end and is the strongest of all.

Once Jupitermon is defeated, he will reappear as Jupitermon WM.

This battle while difficult isn't particularly hard if you have faced the final boss or defeated the Royal Knights, AOEs are your friend in this battle.

When they are defeated there is nothing more left for us to do in this playthrough. Thank you so much for reading!

## Walkthrough

### New Game+

#### About NG+

Once the game is beaten and you select New Game Plus from the menu you can begin the game again from the start but carrying over:

  1. Digimon Scan Rate
  2. Agent Skills
  3. Anomaly Points (if any)
  4. All Items besides key items
  5. Money
  6. and Cards



In addition to the above:

  1. The Digimon Field Guide
  2. Digimon in your party and box and their stats/levels.
  3. Costumes



Side quests and Chests will also reset. Outer dungeon unlock/completion progress is also reset

Mega and Mega+ Difficulty will now be unlocked.

Mega will have enemies scaled to be very difficult where as Mega + will increase it further remove the ability to escape battles or use items in battle. DigiAttacks will also no longer one shot enemies due to their significantly higher levels.

You will not gain more money or Exp despite them being stronger.

Remember difficulty can still be changed back and forth with no limitations.

For reference here are the stats of the first enemy you face in prologue:

![](/ffaq/40/298/)

Another difference is you can now fight and defeat the Chaosdramon in the prologue and he will drop a Talent Awakening Booster DX.

Finally here is a look at your first boss:

![](/ffaq/40/299/)

#### Making Aegiomon Stronger for Higher Difficulties and NG+

Up until recently Aegiomon was thought to be near impossible to make strong enough for Mega and Mega+ as he only gained blue stats from in story evolutions, and could not be trained on the Farm or with chips.

However, the below video explains an exploit to stack his blue stats by constantly restarting NG+ successively. 

Follow the below instructions if you want to max out his stats for NG+

Completing the game on Mega+ will also be the last achievement you complete for this game. You can obtain this by just facing the final boss and playing the rest at a lower difficulty.

## Mega+ Guide

#### What to expect on Mega+

On Mega+ difficulty all enemies have over 9999 ATK and INT stats, the game will show these stats as 9999 but when debuffed the number will never go down. In practice this means most enemies can one shot your digimon without much trouble and if they have advantages such as type or element types it can be fatal.

Most enemies also have high speeds ranging from 4000 to 7000 with certain bosses having over 9999 Speed.

In terms of defensive stats GENERALLY they have defense/spirit around 3000 to 5000 however, towards the end game many bosses will have Defense at 5000 to 7000. Lastly nearly all bosses will have HP ranging from 90k to 300k. 

You will be unable to use items so your ability to heal during battle is limited to skills and personality skills etc.

To make a long story short everything is harder to kill, but can kill you more easily.

It is for this reason you will have to optimize your builds and set ups to ensure your digimon have around max stats or at least around 5000 to 6000.

What is most important is to pay attention to enemy type and weakness. for mobs you can avoid them or just brute force them, but for bosses you MUST pay attention to their type and elements, and use buffs and debuffs effectively.

Below I have included a video of my Mega+ run where I demonstrate how to defeat Mega+ Boss with one attack, use the set ups in the below video as guidance in how you should set up your digimon or how you should play when facing these enemies.

## Akashic Backdoors I (DLC)

#### How to access the Backdoor:

To begin the DLC storyline, you must have bought the DLC or the season pass and entered the elevator in the Theater In Between.

Digimon related to each individual door will be added to your field guide once you do so. You can evolve into them immediately without needing to complete the episode.

Upon entering you are given an Exit Ampule which returns you to the theater. However using it will reset your progress for the area.  
You can find it at the top of the special items tab in the inventory.

#### Akashic Backdoor I

For this mission Mirei will be serving as our temporary Operator..........oh boy. You also do not have access to Aegiomon while here.

##### About the new digimon:

BlitzGreymon and CresGarurumon evolve from MetalGreymon and WereGarurumon respectively.  
The two can fuse into Omnimon Alter S, and Alter S can mode change to Omnimon Alter B.

Omnimon Zwart can now become Omnimon Zwart Defeat, which can then evolve to Omnimon Alter B.

Parallelmon can be evolved from Infermon,Dracomon.

##### A Strange Encounter

Once ready to proceed enter the door in front of you for a scene.

After the scene you will be in a seemingly endless hall way. Keep going forward until you see a door on the side, enter and if you've played Cyber Sleuth you'll meet a familiar face.

This is Kyoko Kuremi, a major character from Cyber Sleuth, though this version is apparently still a teenager.

After the scene you can examine the bubbles, leave the room and another scene will play.

Here we learn an alarming fact, this Kyoko is from a timeline where the Shinjuku Inferno has already occurred.

In any case make your through the hall to another door, once you enter the hallway door will close. Enter the door inside the room.

Get ready for a boss fight:

You will find your self at the Shinjuku Vision Plaza with an angry Goldramon nearby. This won't be just a single boss but up to 3.

##### Goldramon, Megidramon and MirageGaogamon

Goldramon: Vaccine, Holy, Dragon. He is Very weak to Dark and weak to Null, he resists earth and is immune to Light.

Megidramon: Virus: Evil, Dragon. He is weak to ice and water and resists fire and dark.

Lastly, MirageGaogamon: Data, Beast, Knight. Weak to Plant and Elec and resists Metal and Null.

Its handy to have digimon of different types to swap in for this fight.

Once you have beaten the first two, Aegiomon will join as an Uncontrollable guest.

After a rather long fight, you will learn this Inori and Aegiomon are from a different timeline as well explaining why you cannot control Aegiomon.

Though this Aegiomon is a guest he functions like a normal guest mon.

Before you do as Kyoko says and run away, though there are new chests to be found here, also there are wandering enemies you can fight.

Digimon Found  
---  
Gaogamon  
GeoGreymon  
Chrinmon  
  
The first chest is near the ice cream stand to the north of the area next to the building, it contains Revival Capsule DX.

![](/ffaq/40/328/)

The 2nd is found just behind you near the Geogreymon, it contains an HP Casule III.

![](/ffaq/40/329/)

Make your way to the objective, by entering the station. There are two more chests here:

The first is next to the elevators and contains a SP Augment Chip II.

![](/ffaq/40/330/)

The 2nd is next to the gates and the door, it contains an Emerald.

![](/ffaq/40/331/)

Go through the door to return to the corridor. Keep going until you reach a door inside a room filled with people in bubbles.

Once again through the door. This time we'll be outside the Tokyo Metropolitan building. There are 2 chests here too:

The first is right behind you next to the plants, it contains Multi Boost Beta.

![](/ffaq/40/332/)

The next one is across the street next to the door, it contains an HP Capsule III.

![](/ffaq/40/333/)

Go through the door and you will have a scene and CresGarurumon and BlitzGreymon will join up with you.

Continue down the hallway and we'll have another battle, this time against a group of Gatomon and Nefertimon.

They are all Holy, Beast types who are weak to Dark so use that to your advantage.

Not a hard fight, beat them and go through the door.

Here we will receive confirmation that Kodai Kuremi is Kyoko's father.

This version of events is interestingly different from that in Cyber Sleuth where Kyoko's father is already dead and mentioned only as an aside.

As you will now be in Shinjuku park there are a few more chests here:

The first is next to a tree on your right, it contains a Medical Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/334/)

Another chest is to the left of the door on the left end of the park it contains, Multi Recovery.

![](/ffaq/40/335/)

Open the door and continue to the next area. You will now be in Kosuke's office, after the scenes there is a chest in the middle of the room it contains a Revival Capsule.

![](/ffaq/40/336/)

Open the door to continue.

##### Waterway

Digimon Found

WarGrowlmon| Paildramon  
Monochromon| MegaGargomon  
Minotarumon|   
Wingdramon|   
  
You will now be in the Shinjuku Underground Waterway. There are digimon wandering down here too.

Follow the path and you'll see stairs going up, follow them to a dead end with a chest containing Friendship DX.

![](/ffaq/40/337/)

Go back and follow the path until you see a turn going left, follow it to another chest at a dead end, this one has a Talent Awakening Booster.

![](/ffaq/40/338/)

Go back and follow the path straight, until you can exit to the next area.

You will then find yourself inside Rebellion Village, go straight to find a chest part way through, it contains DigiMeat.

![](/ffaq/40/339/)

Go around from the right, and check an alley near the north end for a chest, it has an attack Augment Chip.

![](/ffaq/40/340/)

Continue to the north end to the ladder, go up and check to the left to find another chest, this one has a Diamond.

![](/ffaq/40/341/)

Go up the stairs to the left all the way to the upper deck, once up here her turn around to and go to the other side of the deck to find a chest, this one contains an Int Augment Chip II.

![](/ffaq/40/342/)

When ready enter the door in the center island, get ready to be thrown into a boss fight immediately though.

##### Lanamon (Redux)

Always the pain in the butt. Lanamon is the same as her normal story counterpart but this one is in boss form. She buffs herself at the start by buffing her ATK, INT and Crit. As before she is very weak to Elec but as a Variable type she has no type weakness but she has Pixie and Aquatic traits if that helps.

After you beat her human form she will shift to Beast form, this is once again identical to her boss fight but she will IMMEDIATELY start using her powerful attacks at the start. Everything else is the same.

This fight can be tricky but it doable.

When you regain control you will be outside Kabukicho during the attack on Shinjuku.

There are 2 chests here, first to your left near a table, it contains Full Revive Spray.

![](/ffaq/40/343/)

And there is another chest to your north near another table, it contains SP Capsule III.

![](/ffaq/40/344/)

Then enter Kabukicho for some more chests, the first one is near the entrance of the building to the north, it contains Revival Capsule DX.

![](/ffaq/40/345/)

Then there is another chest near a sign on the left side of the area, it contains Revival Capsule DX

![](/ffaq/40/346/)

Then go to the large open space, and you'll find one more in front of the closest Lion Statue, it contains Courage Point IV.

![](/ffaq/40/347/)

Make your way to the other end of the arcade and you'll find another chest near the vending machines and trash cans, for another Revival Capsule DX.

![](/ffaq/40/348/)

Return back outside to where all the guards are and find the door near the coffee shop entrance, go through to the next area.

You'll now be in the park again with new chests, the first is near the tree to the left in front of you, it contains another Revival Capsule DX.

![](/ffaq/40/349/)

The next one is to the north west near the door it contains, Medical Spray DX.

![](/ffaq/40/350/)

Now go through the door and we're back in the hallways.

Continue on the path and you'll reach anothe room but it's guarded by 3 Mistymon.

They are weak to Elec and are Virus Types. Shouldn't be too hard.

Once they are beaten proceed through the door for more scenes.

When you regain control you will be in the base where the giant is kept, turn around to find a chest in the corner it contains, X Aura.

![](/ffaq/40/351/)

Go to the other end of the room, to find another chest next to the door, this one contains a Multi Recovery.

![](/ffaq/40/352/)

Opposite of the door is a walkway with another chest at the end, this one has a Soul Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/353/)

Enter the door for more scenes.

##### Post Shinjuku Inferno Metropolitan Building

You are now back to where you were in the prologue of the game, neat.

To your right outside the building is a chest, it contains a full revival spray.

![](/ffaq/40/354/)

The building is full of high leveled digimon from when you first visited, minus the Chaosdramon.

After entering the building go down the right hallway to a dead end with a chest, this chest contains a Talent Awakening Booster

![](/ffaq/40/355/)

Return to the center area and make your way through to just before the elevator and go straight through the hall to a dead end with another chest, this one has an Immortal Egg.

![](/ffaq/40/356/)

Take the elevator up.

This area is also the same as the prologue minus the new digimon and chests.

Go down the first hall for a chest containing a Digi Jewel.

![](/ffaq/40/357/)

Make your way to where you fell down with Biyomon to find the door, but go to where you FOUND Biyomon for another chest this one contains an SPI Augment Chip II.

![](/ffaq/40/358/)

Then go back to the door, you will find another chest behind it, this contains a DEF Augment Chip II.

![](/ffaq/40/359/)

Go through the door and you'll be outside the wall of hope.

Go south towards the black truck to find a chest, this one contains an EVA Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/360/)

Next go to the north west end of the area near the yellow bulldozer, for another chest containing Dark Guard.

![](/ffaq/40/361/)

You will find one last chest, near the barriers opposite the previous chest, this one has an Attack Reflect.

![](/ffaq/40/362/)

Then go through the door for a scene.

After the scenes go through the next door.

You will be in the usual hallways again, but there are Wizardmon and Socerermons wandering them to get in the way.

Once you reach the next door, the guard is asleep, so you can head on in.

After these scenes move through the hall and you will arrive at a final door. Once you open it a boss fight is ahead so get ready.

##### Omnimon Zwart Defeat

This won't be easy. This is basically a Royal Knight fight.

He is weak to Light and is a Virus type.Several weaknesses and resistances are different for the boss version. He is resistant to Null instead of being weak to it, and is resistant to fire.

He is around the same level of strength of other enemies but doesn't have any unique boss mechanics beyond getting a bunch of turns and hitting hard.

After the scenes that follow CresGarurumon and BlitzGreymon will fuse into Omnimon Alter S. You will then fight the evolved Alter B.

##### Omnimon Alter B

This version of Alter B is slightly different from the version you can own. It is not weak to light as is immune to dark instead of resisting it. It is still weak to Earth and also resists Null but is neutral to ice. Everything else matches up.

As for any unique aspects for this boss, it likes to buff itself as it loses HP so rather than reducing HP slowly your better served dealing Large chunks of damage, try to take it down in 2 shots to avoid letting it buff up it's easier than it sounded if you focus on it's weaknesses.

If you lower its HP too slowly it will buff up all stats to maximum and apply safety guard as well to stay alive at 1 HP up to 2 times.

After the battle Alter B will join you as a guest mon as well.

When ready to proceed go through the door.

After the scene speak to Kyoko and you'll get Kyoko's Goggles (Spare), a relatively decent equipment item.

When ready to proceed to the final boss of this DLC quest open the last door.

#### Paralllelmon

Parallelmon is classified with the Unknown Trait, and is weak to Light, and is a Virus.

At the start of the battle he will summon Gaiomon and Kuzuhamon (it's a NextOrder reference).   
Kuzuhamon is weak to dark and is a data type, while Gaiomon is a Virus weak to Light and null.

Gaiomon will buff himself and attack multiple times, Kuzuhamon will acts offense and support, after Parallelmon takes enough damage she will buff the party so they take less physical and magic damage.

As Parallelmon takes damage they will get more turns, buff up or debuff the party in required turns.

All 3 are bosses in their own right. But if Parallelmon goes down they all do.

There are 2 ways I recommend approaching this fight.

Either you defeat Parallelmon very quickly like in 1 or 2 hits or you defeat his adds first. Focus on one at a time is safer but each of these guys hit hard so maybe a faster strategy is better. Up to you.

Once the battle is over, the DLC quest will wrap up and you'll get your not very good rewards.

#### Mega+ Akashic Backdoor I

The below video shows how to one shot all bosses and mandatory fights in this DLC, use it as a guide if you are struggling with any of the bosses on Mega+.

## Akashic Backdoors II (DLC)

#### Return of Parallelmon

Back here again are we.

With this DLC all the new digimon should now be available for you to evolve into even without playing the new episode.

The digimon this time are: BanchoStingmon, BanchoLillymon, BanchoMamemon, BanchoGolemon and Omnimon Merciful Mode.

Below are the evolution paths.

From| To  
---|---  
Jewelbeemon| BanchoStingmon  
Lilymon| BanchoLillymon  
Mamemon| BanchoMamemon  
Golemon| BanchoGolemon  
Omnimon| Omnimon Merciful Mode  
  
When ready to begin the episode return to the elevator hall and enter the middle door to view this episode.

As with the first door, you are given an Exit Ampule and using it will allow you to leave but will force you to start over again with all your progress.

You will not have access to the Digifarm for the duration of the episode. You will also not have Aegiomon as a guest.

As with the other door it's scaled to end game difficulty.

After the opening scenes with Mirei enter the door in front of you.

#### Strange Hallway

Digimon Found  
---  
Lucemon  
  
When you go through you will find yourself in a red hall with many doors and Lucemon wandering around (rookie forms). They ignore you even on passing them so no need to fight them.

The doors here lead back to the same place. Use Analyze to identify the correct door (it will be highlighted) it's the on the right in the back.

You will be brought to an identical room, again use analyze and choose the highlighted door.

In the 3rd room is a large door in the back go through.

Then watch the scenes.

You will then be joined by Hiroko and BanchoLeomon.

When ready to proceed go through the door and return to the Strange Hallway for a scene.

Parallelmon will appear and walk towards you slowly, if you are caught you get sent back to the White room.

Enter the door to the left and then continue going through the door on the left 2 times.

You will then be in a room without Parallelmon. Go to the back of the room and take the left exit 2 times again.

Eventually you will be behind Parallelmon with the large door in front of you, go through to proceed.

##### Circular Hallway

There is a door nearby enter it, if you attempt to go around the hall, Parallelmon will appear to chase you and force you to hide in the room.

Once inside enter the Outer Dungeon portal. Get ready for a boss fight.

#### BanchoLillymon

BanchoLillymon is a data type. She is weak to fire and metal. Her trait is pixie.  
Use physical attacks as her SPI is very high plus she also has reduced magic damage buff active.

She gets 3 turns in a row but generally uses AOE attacks.

It's not a hard fight, as long as you have a good physical hitter. For example Beelzemon or Gundramon.

Some scenes will then play.

After that you'll back in the circular hallway.

Make your way out and Parallelmon will pop to chase you, evade him until you find an open door and go inside.  
Doe not enter the door instead keep going around the circle to find an invisible chest.

It contains Safety Guard.

![](/ffaq/40/894/)

Then enter the outer dungeon portal again.You will have another boss fight here.

#### BanchoStingmon

He is a Free type, and is weak to Water and Ice. Trait is Insectoid. He resists Fire and Elec.

He has powerful AOE and single target skills, he hits hard and one of the faster bosses in the game outside the royal knights.

He has lower HP than BanchoLillymon but hits a lot harder so keep this in mind when planning out your party set up.

For this digimon TigerVespamon is pretty handy, on top of doing damage to insectoids you can do more damage by reversing BanchoStingmon.

He has 4 actions per turn and will often use the last action to buff his speed or use acceleration boost.

When you defeated him watch the scenes.

You will return to the Circular Hallway, look for the next door and enter the Outer Dungeon portal inside, for another boss fight.

#### BanchoGolemon

A Virus type. He is weak to wind and plant but resists earth and metal. This boss also out right resists physical damage. You're going to do more damage with magic skills this time.

Aside from that he is a lot easier than BanchoStingmon and can be beaten by any digimon who has the right elemental set up and magic  
attacks. Debuffing also helps when dealing with his defenses.

He only gets 2 actions per turn so it should be easier for debuffs to last.

Watch the scenes and you will return to the hallway again.

This hallway has another hidden chest just a little bit further from the door it contains Curse Attachment II.

![](/ffaq/40/895/)

Go into the room and go through the portal, will then have another boss.

#### BanchoMamemon

He is a Data type, is weak to Elec and Ice while resisting Metal and Null. It has the Mineral Trait.

He has his crit rate buffed. Aside from that this is a pretty easy boss, he mainly uses AOE attacks.

Using High field alongside TigerVespamon makes this fight pretty easy overall.

After the fight some scenes will play, we will be back in the first room. Omnimon will have joined the party.

#### Choose Your Bancho

Now you must choose which of the remaining Bancho you have fought will join the final Guest spot.

Once you select them, it's time to head out.

BEFORE you do, speak to Hiroko to get a unique item, Hiroko's Memory Card. It gives you 1000 HP and lowers Virus damage by 10%.  
Not a great item frankly.

#### Strange Hallway 2 and Circular Hallway

Digimon Found  
---  
Valkyrimon  
  
The hall has some enemies you can use to build your your CP or any other set up you need for the upcoming boss.

When ready to proceed go through the door to reach the circular hall again.

Now you must go into one of the 4 rooms with the dark crystals. Each room will be filled with tiny Parallelmons, if they touch you there will be  
a battle.

That said they can all be defeated instantly with digiattacks. But be careful they regenerate very quickly, if you hear a loud warping sound quickly go to the crystal.

Repeat this process with all 4 crystals and watch the scenes.

After the 2nd time Mini-Parallelmons will appear in the halls too.

Once all the scenes pass, Omnimon will evolve to Merciful Mode.

Make your way to the final door and the final battle of this DLC.

#### Parallelmon 2

Just as the previous time he was fought Parallelmon will summon two companion digimon.

This time he calls Darkdramon and Tankdramon.

As before Parallelmon is a Virus and is weak to Light and resists Null.

Tankdramon is a Virus weak to Elec and Water. Darkdramon is a Virus weak to Null and Elec.

As with the first version of this fight. It's better to ignore his adds and defeat him directly.

Just like the previous version this fight becomes more dangerous the longer you let Parallelmon remain active, the lower his HP gets the more buffed his allies, unlike last time both are primarily more offensive and don't buff Parallelmon but gain buffs the lower his HP.

If Parallelmon gains even 1 CP he will buff his defenses. It is best to debuff him and use a digimon like Seraphimon to deal big damage with Seven stars a few times to finish him before things get hairy (High field is your friend).

Once you have defeated him enjoy the remaining scenes of this episode and it's time to say goodbye once again. Until the next episode.

Clearing this will give you Talent Awakening Booster Ex: 02 and 300K Yen.

## Field Guide: Basics

#### Overview

This section will serve as a fully readable field guide listing information about digimon in the game for every stage.  
There are 451 digimon in the game and 475 including DLC.

This section will list and connect each one giving you access to their information whenever you need them even if you don't have access to that info in game.

##### About Digimon Information:

  *  **Attribute** : Typically these identify digimon in their type triangle as Virus, Vaccine and Data. They obey the below triangle of however  
certain attributes such as Free, Variable, Unknown and No Data exist outside the triangle and hence do not interact.



![](/ffaq/40/363/)

  *  **Generation** : This refers to the evolution stage and extends from In-Training I to Mega+ at the highest. Some digimon exist in special generations however the game and this guide treats them as a stage equivalent in strength (for example most Armor digimon are treated as Champion digimon).
  *  **Stats** : Self explanatory, as the stat numbers of digimon can be highly variable due to level, training, personality and agent skills, this section will only cover the digimon's BASE STATS at level 1 and 99. **Important: Level 99 stats are based on the digimon remaining in its base personality change in personality will slightly alter stat gain.**
  *  **Hidden Stats:** All digimon have 3 hidden stats Crit Rate, Accuracy and Evasion. Crit rate of most skills is by default 5% with bond and equipment or buffs increasing it, certain skills have higher crit rates. Accuracy tends to be 100 for all skills unless stated otherwise. Evasion can only be increased by buffs and equipment.
  *  **Base Personality** : This is the personality the digimon is LIKELY to have upon conversion this is only the highest percentage and not the only possibility.
  *  **Elemental Resistances:** These are the digimon's elemental weaknesses and resistances. The below icons reference how much damage elemental resistances apply.

Icon| Damage multiplier  
---|---  
![](/ffaq/40/1426/)|  **2x**  
![](/ffaq/40/1427/)|  **1.5x**  
![](/ffaq/40/1421/)| 

 **1x**  
  
![](/ffaq/40/1423/)|  **0.5x**  
![](/ffaq/40/1428/)|  **0x**  
  
  *  **Special Skills:** These are the signature skills unique to specific digimon, aside from the elemental and attribute resistances and physical appearance this is the only unique aspect of any digimon.
  *  **Learnable Attachment Skills** : These are the attachment skills a digimon can learn, these skills can be removed and equipped freely and are treated as items to place in your inventory when obtained at certain levels.
  *  **Traits:** All digimon have traits, which identify it based on physical appearance or characteristic. Certain traits are simply based on size or are based on the role they play in the story, as such certain story enemies will have traits not listed in this guide. However generally digimon special skills have increased damage when targetting digimon of specific traits. Traits cannot be viewed in the in game field guide and only when scanning enemies in battle. So they will be properly listed here for your convenience.
  *  **Ridable** : This is just whether you can ride on a digimon while in the digital world. Note: Different digirides have different speeds.

Below is a full list ordered by number, you can use CTRL+F to find the digimon you want and click the link to view the related digimon's page.

## Privacy Preference Center

When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences, or your device, and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect. The information does not usually identify you directly, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose not to allow some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to learn more and change our default settings. Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer.   
Allow All

###  Manage Consent Preferences

#### Strictly Necessary Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. They are usually only set in response to actions made by you which amount to a request for services, such as setting your privacy preferences, logging in or filling in forms. You can set your browser to block or alert you about these cookies, but some parts of the site will not then work. These cookies do not store any personally identifiable information.

#### Performance Cookies

Always Active

These cookies allow us to count visits and traffic sources so we can measure and improve the performance of our site. They help us to know which pages are the most and least popular and see how visitors move around the site. All information these cookies collect is aggregated and therefore anonymous. If you do not allow these cookies we will not know when you have visited our site, and will not be able to monitor its performance.

#### Functional Cookies

Always Active

These cookies enable the website to provide enhanced functionality and personalisation. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. If you do not allow these cookies then some or all of these services may not function properly.

#### Targeting Cookies

Always Active

These cookies may be set through our site by our advertising partners. They may be used by those companies to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant adverts on other sites. They do not store directly personal information, but are based on uniquely identifying your browser and internet device. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less targeted advertising.

#### Social Media Cookies

Always Active

These cookies are set by a range of social media services that we have added to the site to enable you to share our content with your friends and networks. They are capable of tracking your browser across other sites and building up a profile of your interests. This may impact the content and messages you see on other websites you visit. If you do not allow these cookies you may not be able to use or see these sharing tools.
